question: $$	ext{If } \left( a ight) \left( x - 1 ight) = \left( b ight) \left( x - 3 ight), 	ext{ then the value of } x 	ext{ is:}$$ A. $$ rac{1}{2}$$ B. $$1$$ C. $$2$$ D. $$ rac{7}{2}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$ 	ext{Given } \left(  egin{array}{c} a \ x - 1 \end{array} ight) = \left(  egin{array}{c} b \ x - 3 \end{array} ight) \div \left(  egin{array}{c} b \ a \end{array} ight) $$

$$ \Rightarrow x - 1 = 3 - x $$

$$ \Rightarrow 2x = 4 $$

$$ \Rightarrow x = 2. $$

question: $$	ext{If } 3^{(x - y)} = 27 	ext{ and } 3^{(x + y)} = 243, 	ext{ then } x 	ext{ is equal to:}$$ A. $$0$$ B. $$2$$ C. $$4$$ D. $$6$$

$$	ext{3}^{	ext{x} + 	ext{y}} = 243 = 3^5 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 	ext{x} + 	ext{y} = 5 ....(ii)$$
$$	ext{On solving (i) and (ii), we get x = 4.}$$

question: $$	ext{8.} \quad  rac{1}{1 + x^{(b - a)} + x^{(c - a)}} +  rac{1}{1 + x^{(a - b)} + x^{(c - b)}} +  rac{1}{1 + x^{(b - c)} + x^{(a - c)}} = ?$$ A. $$0$$ B. $$1$$ C. $$	ext{x}^{	ext{a} - 	ext{b} - 	ext{c}}$$ D. $$	ext{None of these}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Given Exp. =}  rac{1}{x^a} +  rac{1}{x^b} +  rac{1}{x^c} =  rac{x^a + x^b + x^c}{(x^a + x^b + x^c)(x^a + x^b + x^c)} = 1.$$

question:
$$	ext{(243)}^{ rac{n}{5}} 	imes 3^{2n + 1} = ?$$
$$	ext{9}^{n} 	imes 3^{n - 1}$$ A. $$1$$ B. $$	ext{2}$$ C. $$9$$ D. $$	ext{3}^{n}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: ```tex
$$
 egin{aligned}
&	ext{Given Expression} \
&\quad =  rac{(243)^{n/5} 	imes 3^{2n + 1}}{9^n 	imes 3^{n - 1}} \
&\quad =  rac{(3^5)^{n/5} 	imes 3^{2n + 1}}{(3^2)^n 	imes 3^{n - 1}} \
&\quad =  rac{3^{5 	imes (n/5)} 	imes 3^{2n + 1}}{3^{2n} 	imes 3^{n - 1}} \
&\quad =  rac{3^n 	imes 3^{2n + 1}}{3^{2n} 	imes 3^{n - 1}} \
&\quad =  rac{3^{n + 2n + 1}}{3^{2n + n - 1}} \
&\quad =  rac{3^{3n + 1}}{3^{3n - 1}} \
&\quad = 3^{(3n + 1 - 3n + 1)} = 3^2 = 9.
\end{aligned}
$$
```

$$	ext{If } m 	ext{ and } n 	ext{ are whole numbers such that } m^n = 121, 	ext{ the value of }(m - 1)^{n + 1} 	ext{ is:}$$ A. $$1$$ B. $$10$$ C. $$121$$ D. $$1000$$

question: $$ rac{x^b}{(b + c - a)} \cdot  rac{x^c}{(c + a - b)} \cdot  rac{x^a}{(a + b - c)} = ?$$ A. $$x^{abc}$$ B. $$1$$ C. $$x^{ab + bc + ca}$$ D. $$x^{a + b + c}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Given Exp.}= x^{(b - c)(b + c - a)} . x^{(c - a)(c + a - b)} . x^{(a - b)(a + b - c)}$$
$$	ext{Given Exp.}= x^{(b^2 - c^2 + c^2 - a^2 + a^2 - b^2)} . x^{-a(b - c) - b(c - a) - c(a - b)}$$
$$	ext{Given Exp.}= (x^0 \cdot x^0) = (1 \cdot 1) = 1.$$

question: $$	ext{In a mixture 60 litres, the ratio of milk and water 2 : 1. If this ratio is to be 1 : 2, then the quantity of water to be further added is:}$$ A. $$	ext{20 litres}$$ B. $$	ext{30 litres}$$ C. $$	ext{40 litres}$$ D. $$	ext{60 litres}$$

$$	ext{Then, milk : water} =  rac{40}{20 + x}.$$

$$	ext{Therefore, } 20 + x = 80$$

$$	ext{Hence, } x = 60.$$

$$	ext{Quantity of water to be added} = 60 	ext{ litres}.$$

question: $$	ext{7. Salaries of Ravi and Sumit are in the ratio 2 : 3. If the salary of each is increased by Rs. 4000, the new ratio becomes 40 : 57. What is Sumit's salary?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 17,000}$$ B. $$Rs. 20,000$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 25,500}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 38,000}$$

$$Then,$$
$$2x + 4000 =  rac{40}{57}(3x + 4000)$$

$$6x = 68,000$$

$$3x = 34,000$$

$$Sumit's\ present\ salary\ = (3x + 4000) = Rs.(34000 + 4000) = Rs.\ 38,000.$$

question: $$	ext{14. 36 men can complete a piece of work in 18 days. In how many days will 27 men complete the same work?}$$ A. $$12$$ B. $$18$$ C. $$22$$ D. $$	ext{24}$$ E. $$	ext{None of these}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Let the required number of days be } x.$$
$$	ext{Less men, More days (Indirect Proportion)}$$
$$	ext{27 : 36 } :: 18 : x \quad \Rightarrow \quad 27x = 36 	imes 18$$

$$ egin{array}{c|c}
\hline
	ext{Dividend: } 36 	imes 18 & 	ext{Divisor: } 27 \
\hline
\end{array}$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad x = 24.$$

question: $$	ext{15. 4 mat-weavers can weave 4 mats in 4 days. At the same rate, how many mats would be woven by 8 mat-weavers in 8 days?}$$ A. $$4$$ B. $$8$$ C. $$12$$ D. $$	ext{16}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Let the required number of bottles be } x.$$
$$	ext{More weavers, More mats (Direct Proportion)}$$
$$	ext{More days, More mats (Direct Proportion)}$$

$$ egin{array}{ccccc}
	ext{Wavers} & : & 4 & : & 8 \
 & & : & : & \
	ext{Days} & : & 4 & : & 8 \
 & & 	ext{::} & & 4 : x
\end{array}$$

$$4 	imes 4 	imes x = 8 	imes 8 	imes 4$$

$$ egin{array}{cc}
\hline x =  rac{(8 	imes 8 	imes 4)}{(4 	imes 4)} \
\hline
\end{array}$$

$$x = 16.$$

question: $$	ext{Three pipes A, B and C can fill a tank from empty to full in 30 minutes, 20 minutes, and 10 minutes respectively. When the tank is empty, all the three pipes are opened. A, B and C discharge chemical solutions P,Q and R respectively. What is the proportion of the solution R in the liquid in the tank after 3 minutes?}$$ A. $$ rac{5}{11}$$ B. $$ rac{6}{11}$$ C. $$ rac{7}{11}$$ D. $$ rac{8}{11}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Part filled by (A + B + C) in 3 minutes = 3}\left( rac{1}{30} +  rac{1}{20} +  rac{1}{10}ight) = 3\left( rac{11}{60}ight) =  rac{11}{20}.$$

$$	ext{Part filled by C in 3 minutes = } rac{3}{10}.$$

$$	ext{Required ratio = }\left( rac{3}{10}ight) 	imes 20 = 6.$$

question: $$	ext{Pipes A and B can fill a tank in 5 and 6 hours respectively. Pipe C can empty it in 12 hours. If all the three pipes are opened together, then the tank will be filled in:}$$ A. $$ rac{13 	ext{ hours}}{17}$$ B. $$ rac{2 	imes 8}{11} 	ext{ hours}$$ C. $$ rac{3 	imes 9}{17} 	ext{ hours}$$ D. $$ rac{4}{2} 	ext{ hours}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$
	ext{Net part filled in 1 hour} = \left(  rac{1}{5} +  rac{1}{6} -  rac{1}{12} ight) =  rac{17}{60}
$$

$$
	ext{The tank will be full in} =  rac{60}{17} 	ext{ hours} pprox 3 rac{9}{17} 	ext{ hours}.
$$

question: $$	ext{3. A pump can fill a tank with water in 2 hours. Because of a leak, it took 2} rac{1}{3}	ext{ hours to fill the tank. The leak can drain all the water of the tank in:}$$ A. $$ rac{4}{3} 	ext{ hours}$$ B. $$	ext{7 hours}$$ C. $$	ext{8 hours}$$ D. $$	ext{14 hours}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$Work\ done\ by\ the\ leak\ in\ 1\ hour\ = \left( rac{1}{3} -  rac{1}{2}ight) =  rac{1}{14}$$

$$Leak\ will\ empty\ the\ tank\ in\ 14\ hrs.$$

question: $$	ext{Two pipes A and B can fill a cistern in 37} rac{1}{2}	ext{ minutes and 45 minutes respectively. Both pipes are opened. The cistern will be filled in just half an hour, if the B is turned off after:}$$ A. $$	ext{5 min.}$$ B. $$	ext{9 min.}$$ C. $$	ext{10 min.}$$ D. $$	ext{15 min.}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Let B be turned off after } x 	ext{ minutes. Then,}$$
$$	ext{Part filled by (A + B) in } x 	ext{ min. + Part filled by A in (30 -} x 	ext{) min. = 1.}$$
$$ rac{x}{2 + 1} + (30 - x) \cdot  rac{2}{75} = 1$$
$$\Rightarrow  rac{x}{3} +  rac{60 - 2x}{75} = 1$$
$$\Rightarrow 25x + 180 - 6x = 225$$
$$\Rightarrow 19x = 45$$
$$\Rightarrow x = 9$$

question: $$	ext{A tank is filled by three pipes with uniform flow. The first two pipes operating simultaneously fill the tank in the same time during which the tank is filled by the third pipe alone. The second pipe fills the tank 5 hours faster than the first pipe and 4 hours slower than the third pipe. The time required by the first pipe is:}$$ A. $$	ext{6 hours}$$ B. $$	ext{10 hours}$$ C. $$	ext{15 hours}$$ D. $$	ext{30 hours}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Suppose, first pipe alone takes } x 	ext{ hours to fill the tank.}$$
$$	ext{Then, second and third pipes will take } (x - 5) 	ext{ and } (x - 9) 	ext{ hours respectively to fill the tank.}$$

$$ egin{array}{ccccc}
 rac{1}{x} & + &  rac{1}{x - 5} & = &  rac{1}{x - 9} \
\end{array}$$

$$ egin{array}{ccc}
 rac{x - 5 + x}{x(x - 5)} & = &  rac{1}{x - 9} \
\end{array}$$

$$(2x - 5)(x - 9) = x(x - 5)$$
$$x^2 - 18x + 45 = 0$$
$$(x - 15)(x - 3) = 0$$
$$x = 15 	ext{ [neglecting } x = 3]$$

question: $$	ext{Two pipes can fill a tank in 20 and 24 minutes respectively and a waste pipe can empty 3 gallons per minute. All the three pipes working together can fill the tank in 15 minutes. The capacity of the tank is:}$$ A. $$	ext{60 gallons}$$ B. $$	ext{100 gallons}$$ C. $$	ext{120 gallons}$$ D. $$	ext{180 gallons}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Work done by the waste pipe in 1 minute} =  rac{1}{15} -  rac{1}{20} +  rac{1}{24}$$

$$	ext{Work done by the waste pipe in 1 minute} =  rac{1}{15} -  rac{11}{120}$$

$$	ext{Work done by the waste pipe in 1 minute} = - rac{1}{40} 	ext{ (negative sign means emptying)}$$

$$	ext{Volume of 1 part} = 3 	ext{ gallons}$$

$$	ext{Volume of whole} = (3 	imes 40) 	ext{ gallons} = 120 	ext{ gallons}$$

question: $$	ext{7. A tank is filled in 5 hours by three pipes A, B and C. The pipe C is twice as fast as B and B is twice as fast as A. How much time will pipe A alone take to fill the tank?}$$ A. $$	ext{20 hours}$$ B. $$	ext{25 hours}$$ C. $$	ext{35 hours}$$ D. $$	ext{Cannot be determined}$$ E. $$	ext{None of these}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$Suppose\ pipe\ A\ alone\ takes\ 	ext{x hours to fill the tank}.$$

$$Then,\ pipes\ B\ and\ C\ will\ take\  rac{	ext{x}}{2}\ and\  rac{	ext{x}}{4}\ hours\ respectively\ to\ fill\ the tank.$$

$$ rac{1}{	ext{x}} +  rac{2}{	ext{x}} +  rac{4}{	ext{x}} =  rac{1}{5}$$

$$ rac{7}{	ext{x}} =  rac{1}{5}$$

$$	ext{x} = 35\ hrs.$$

question: $$	ext{8. Two pipes A and B together can fill a cistern in 4 hours. Had they been opened separately, then B would have taken 6 hours more than A to fill the cistern. How much time will be taken by A to fill the cistern separately?}$$ A. $$	ext{1 hour}$$ B. $$	ext{2 hours}$$ C. $$	ext{6 hours}$$ D. $$	ext{8 hours}$$

$$	ext{9. Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 and 30 minutes respectively. If both the pipes are used together, then how long will it take to fill the tank?}$$ A. $$	ext{12 min}$$ B. $$	ext{15 min}$$ C. $$	ext{25 min}$$ D. $$	ext{50 min}$$
answer:
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Part filled by A in 1 min} =  rac{1}{20}$$

$$	ext{Part filled by B in 1 min} =  rac{1}{30}$$

$$	ext{Part filled by (A + B) in 1 min} = \left( rac{1}{20} +  rac{1}{30}ight) =  rac{1}{12}$$

$$	ext{Both pipes can fill the tank in 12 minutes}.$$

question: $$	ext{10. Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 15 minutes and 20 minutes respectively. Both the pipes are opened together but after 4 minutes, pipe A is turned off. What is the total time required to fill the tank?}$$ A. $$	ext{10 min. 20 sec.}$$ B. $$11 	ext{ min.} 45 	ext{ sec.}$$ C. $$12 	ext{ min.} 30 	ext{ sec.}$$ D. $$	ext{14 min. 40 sec.}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$
	ext{Part filled in 4 minutes} = 4 \left( rac{1}{15} +  rac{1}{20}ight) = 7  rac{1}{15}
$$

$$
	ext{Remaining part} = 1 - 7  rac{1}{15} = 8  rac{1}{15}
$$

$$
	ext{Part filled by B in 1 minute} =  rac{1}{20}
$$

$$
	ext{Ratio} =  rac{1}{20} :  rac{8}{15} \Rightarrow 1 : x
$$

$$
x =  rac{8}{15} 	imes 1 	imes 20 = 10  rac{2}{3} 	ext{ min} = 10 	ext{ min. 40 sec.}
$$

$$
	ext{The tank will be full in} (4 	ext{ min.} + 10 	ext{ min.} + 40 	ext{ sec.}) = 14 	ext{ min. 40 sec.}
$$

question: $$	ext{11. One pipe can fill a tank three times as fast as another pipe. If together the two pipes can fill the tank in 36 minutes, then the slower pipe alone will be able to fill the tank in:}$$ A. $$	ext{81 min.}$$ B. $$	ext{108 min.}$$ C. $$	ext{144 min.}$$ D. $$	ext{192 min.}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Let the slower pipe alone fill the tank in } x 	ext{ minutes.}$$

$$	ext{Then, faster pipe will fill it in }  rac{x}{3} 	ext{ minutes.}$$

$$ rac{1}{x} +  rac{1}{3} =  rac{1}{36}$$

$$ rac{4}{x} =  rac{1}{36}$$

$$x = 144 	ext{ min.}$$

question: $$	ext{12. A large tanker can be filled by two pipes A and B in 60 minutes and 40 minutes respectively. How many minutes will it take to fill the tanker from empty state if B is used for half the time and A and B fill it together for the other half?}$$ A. $$	ext{15 min}$$ B. $$	ext{20 min}$$ C. $$	ext{27.5 min}$$ D. $$	ext{30 min}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Part filled by (A + B) in 1 minute} = \left( rac{1}{60} +  rac{1}{40}ight) =  rac{1}{24}$$

$$	ext{Suppose the tank is filled in } x 	ext{ minutes.}$$

$$	ext{Then, } x \left( rac{1}{24} +  rac{1}{40}ight) = 1$$

$$\Rightarrow x \left( rac{1}{15}ight) = 1$$

$$\Rightarrow x = 30 	ext{ min.}$$

question: $$	ext{13. A tap can fill a tank in 6 hours. After half the tank is filled, three more similar taps are opened. What is the total time taken to fill the tank completely?}$$ A. $$3 	ext{ hrs } 15 	ext{ min}$$ B. $$	ext{3 hrs 45 min}$$ C. $$	ext{4 hrs}$$ D. $$	ext{4 hrs 15 min}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: Here is the HTML content converted into TeX format:

```tex
$$
	ext{Time taken by one tap to fill half of the tank} = 3 	ext{ hrs}.
$$

$$
	ext{Part filled by the four taps in 1 hour} = \left(4 	imes  rac{1}{3}ight) = 2.
$$

$$
	ext{Remaining part} = \left(1 -  rac{1}{2}ight) =  rac{1}{2}.
$$

$$
 rac{2}{3} :  rac{1}{2} :: 1 : x
$$

$$
	ext{Therefore, } x = \left( rac{1}{2} 	imes 1 	imes  rac{3}{4}ight) =  rac{3}{8} 	ext{ hours i.e., 45 mins}.
$$

$$
	ext{So, total time taken} = 3 	ext{ hrs.} 45 	ext{ mins}.
$$
```

question: $$	ext{14. Three taps A, B and C can fill a tank in 12, 15 and 20 hours respectively. If A is open all the time and B and C are open for one hour each alternately, the tank will be full in:}$$ A. $$	ext{6 hours}$$ B. $$ rac{6}{3} 	ext{ hours}$$ C. $$	ext{7 hours}$$ D. $$ rac{7}{2} 	ext{ hours}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$
	ext{(A + B)'s 1 hour's work =} \left( rac{1}{12} +  rac{1}{15}ight) =  rac{9}{60} =  rac{3}{20}.
$$

$$
	ext{(A + C)'s 1 hour's work =} \left( rac{1}{12} +  rac{1}{20}ight) =  rac{8}{60} =  rac{2}{15}.
$$

$$
	ext{Part filled in 2 hrs =} \left( rac{3}{20} +  rac{2}{15}ight) =  rac{17}{60}.
$$

$$
	ext{Part filled in 6 hrs =} 3 	imes  rac{17}{60} =  rac{17}{20}.
$$

$$
	ext{Remaining part =} \left(1 -  rac{17}{20}ight) =  rac{3}{20}.
$$

$$
	ext{Now, it is the turn of A and B and}  rac{3}{20} 	ext{part is filled by A and B in 1 hour.}
$$

$$
	ext{Total time taken to fill the tank = (6 + 1) hrs = 7 hrs.}
$$

question: $$	ext{A man's speed with the current is 15 km/hr and the speed of the current is 2.5 km/hr. The man's speed against the current is:}$$ A. $$	ext{8.5 km/hr}$$ B. $$	ext{9 km/hr}$$ C. $$	ext{10 km/hr}$$ D. $$	ext{12.5 km/hr}$$

question: $$	ext{A boat running upstream takes 8 hours 48 minutes to cover a certain distance, while it takes 4 hours to cover the same distance running downstream. What is the ratio between the speed of the boat and speed of the water current respectively?}$$ A. $$	ext{2 : 1}$$ B. $$ rac{3}{2}$$ C. $$ rac{8}{3}$$ D. $$	ext{Cannot be determined}$$ E. $$	ext{None of these}$$

	ext{Let the man's rate upstream be } x 	ext{ kmph and that downstream be } y 	ext{ kmph.}
$$

$$
	ext{Then, distance covered upstream in 8 hrs 48 min = Distance covered downstream in 4 hrs.}
$$

$$
 rac{x 	imes 8 + 4}{5} = y 	imes 4
$$

$$
 rac{44}{5} x = 4y
$$

$$
y =  rac{11}{5} x
$$

$$
	ext{Required ratio = }  rac{y + x}{2} :  rac{y - x}{2}
$$

$$
	ext{ = }  rac{16x + 1}{5} :  rac{6x + 1}{2}
$$

$$
	ext{ = }  rac{8}{5} :  rac{3}{5}
$$

$$
	ext{ = 8 : 3.}
$$

question: $$	ext{In one hour, a boat goes 11 km/hr along the stream and 5 km/hr against the stream. The speed of the boat in still water (in km/hr) is:}$$ A. $$	ext{3 km/hr}$$ B. $$	ext{5 km/hr}$$ C. $$	ext{8 km/hr}$$ D. $$	ext{9 km/hr}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$ 	ext{Speed in still water} =  rac{1}{2} (11 + 5) 	ext{ kmph} = 8 	ext{ kmph}. $$

question: $$	ext{A boat running downstream covers a distance of 16 km in 2 hours while for covering the same distance upstream, it takes 4 hours. What is the speed of the boat in still water?}$$ A. $$	ext{4 km/hr}$$ B. $$	ext{6 km/hr}$$ C. $$	ext{8 km/hr}$$ D. $$	ext{Data inadequate}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$
	ext{Rate downstream} = \left( rac{16}{2}ight)	ext{kmph} = 8 	ext{ kmph}.
$$

$$
	ext{Rate upstream} = \left( rac{16}{4}ight)	ext{kmph} = 4 	ext{ kmph}.
$$

$$
	ext{Speed in still water} =  rac{1}{2}(8 + 4) 	ext{ kmph} = 6 	ext{ kmph}.
$$

question: $$	ext{The speed of a boat in still water is 15 km/hr and the rate of current is 3 km/hr. The distance travelled downstream in 12 minutes is:}$$ A. $$	ext{1.2 km}$$ B. $$	ext{1.8 km}$$ C. $$	ext{2.4 km}$$ D. $$	ext{3.6 km}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Speed downstream} = (15 + 3) 	ext{ kmph} = 18 	ext{ kmph}.$$

$$	ext{Distance travelled} =  rac{18 	imes 12}{60} 	ext{ km} = 3.6 	ext{ km}.$$

question: $$	ext{A boat takes 90 minutes less to travel 36 miles downstream than to travel the same distance upstream. If the speed of the boat in still water is 10 mph, the speed of the stream is:}$$ A. $$	ext{2 mph}$$ B. $$	ext{2.5 mph}$$ C. $$	ext{3 mph}$$ D. $$	ext{4 mph}$$
answer:
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Let the speed of the stream } x 	ext{ mph. Then,}$$
$$	ext{Speed downstream = }(10 + x) 	ext{ mph,}$$
$$	ext{Speed upstream = }(10 - x) 	ext{ mph.}$$
$$ rac{36}{10 - x} -  rac{36}{10 + x} = 60$$
$$72x \cdot 60 = 90 (100 - x^2)$$
$$x^2 + 48x - 100 = 0$$
$$(x+ 50)(x - 2) = 0$$
$$x = 2 	ext{ mph.}$$

question: $$9.	ext{ A man can row at 5 kmph in still water. If the velocity of current is 1 kmph and it takes him 1 hour to row to a place and come back, how far is the place?}$$ A. $$	ext{2.4 km}$$ B. $$	ext{2.5 km}$$ C. $$	ext{3 km}$$ D. $$	ext{3.6 km}$$
answer:
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Speed downstream} = (5 + 1) 	ext{ kmph} = 6 	ext{ kmph}.$$
$$	ext{Speed upstream} = (5 - 1) 	ext{ kmph} = 4 	ext{ kmph}.$$
$$	ext{Let the required distance be } x 	ext{ km}.$$
$$ rac{x}{6} +  rac{x}{4} = 1$$
$$2x + 3x = 12$$
$$5x = 12$$
$$x = 2.4 	ext{ km}.$$

question: $$	ext{10. A boat covers a certain distance downstream in 1 hour, while it comes back in 1} rac{1}{2}	ext{ hours. If the speed of the stream be 3 kmph, what is the speed of the boat in still water?}$$ A. $$	ext{12 kmph}$$ B. $$	ext{13 kmph}$$ C. $$	ext{14 kmph}$$ D. $$	ext{15 kmph}$$ E. $$	ext{None of these}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Let the speed of the boat in still water be } x 	ext{ kmph. Then,}$$
$$	ext{Speed downstream = } (x + 3) 	ext{ kmph,}$$
$$	ext{Speed upstream = } (x - 3) 	ext{ kmph.}$$

$$ rac{(x + 3) 	imes 1}{(x - 3) 	imes 2} =  rac{3}{2}$$

$$\Rightarrow 2x + 6 = 3x - 9$$
$$\Rightarrow x = 15 	ext{ kmph.}$$

question: $$	ext{11. A boatman goes 2 km against the current of the stream in 1 hour and goes 1 km along the current in 10 minutes. How long will it take to go 5 km in stationary water?}$$ A. $$	ext{40 minutes}$$ B. $$	ext{1 hour}$$ C. $$	ext{1 hr 15 min}$$ D. $$	ext{1 hr 30 min}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$
	ext{Rate downstream} = \left( rac{1}{10} 	imes 60ight) 	ext{km/hr} = 6 	ext{km/hr}.
$$

$$
	ext{Rate upstream} = 2 	ext{km/hr}.

question: $$	ext{12. A man can row three-quarters of a kilometre against the stream in 11} rac{1}{4}	ext{ minutes and down the stream in 7} rac{1}{2}	ext{ minutes. The speed (in km/hr) of the man in still water is:}$$ A. $$	ext{2}$$ B. $$3$$ C. $$4$$ D. $$5$$

$$ =  rac{25}{18} 	ext{ m/sec.} $$

$$ =  rac{25}{18} 	imes  rac{18}{5} 	ext{ km/hr.} $$

$$ = 5 	ext{ km/hr.} $$

question: $$14. 	ext{A man takes twice as long to row a distance against the stream as to row the same distance in favour of the stream. The ratio of the speed of the boat (in still water) and the stream is:}$$ A. $$	ext{2 : 1}$$ B. $$ rac{3}{1}$$ C. $$ rac{3}{2}$$ D. $$ rac{4}{3}$$

question: $$	ext{15. A man rows to a place 48 km distant and comes back in 14 hours. He finds that he can row 4 km with the stream in the same time as 3 km against the stream. The rate of the stream is:}$$ A. $$	ext{1 km/hr}$$ B. $$	ext{1.5 km/hr}$$ C. $$	ext{2 km/hr}$$ D. $$	ext{2.5 km/hr}$$

$$	ext{Rate of the stream} =  rac{1}{2} (8 - 6) 	ext{ km/hr} = 1 	ext{ km/hr.}$$

question: $$	ext{A vessel is filled with liquid, 3 parts of which are water and 5 parts syrup. How much of the mixture must be drawn off and replaced with water so that the mixture may be half water and half syrup?}$$ A. $$ rac{1}{3}$$ B. $$ rac{1}{4}$$ C. $$ rac{1}{5}$$ D. $$ rac{1}{7}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Suppose the vessel initially contains 8 litres of liquid.}$$

$$	ext{Let } x 	ext{ litres of this liquid be replaced with water.}$$

$$	ext{Quantity of water in new mixture = } \left( 3 - 3x + x ight) 	ext{ litres}$$

$$	ext{Quantity of syrup in new mixture = } \left( 5 - 5x ight) 	ext{ litres}$$

$$ rac{3 - 3x + x}{8} =  rac{5 - 5x}{8}$$

$$\Rightarrow 5x + 24 = 40 - 5x$$

$$\Rightarrow 10x = 16$$

$$\Rightarrow x =  rac{8}{5}$$

$$	ext{So, part of the mixture replaced = } \left(  rac{8}{5} 	imes  rac{1}{8} ight) =  rac{1}{5}$$

question: $$	ext{Tea worth Rs. 126 per kg and Rs. 135 per kg are mixed with a third variety in the ratio 1 : 1 : 2. If the mixture is worth Rs. 153 per kg, the price of the third variety per kg will be:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 169.50}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 170}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 175.50}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 180}$$

$$	ext{So, the mixture is formed by mixing two varieties, one at Rs. 130.50 per kg and the other at say, Rs. } x 	ext{ per kg in the ratio 2 : 2, } 	ext{i.e.,} 1 : 1.$$

$$ egin{array}{ccc}

	ext{Rs. 130.50} & & x \
(x - 153) & & 22.50
\end{array}$$

$$ rac{x - 153}{22.50} = 1$$

$$x - 153 = 22.50$$

$$x = 175.50$$

$$
 rac{7x - 21}{4} =  rac{5x - 15}{4} + 9.

question: $$	ext{14. 8 litres are drawn from a cask full of wine and is then filled with water. This operation is performed three more times. question: $$	ext{13. Two cards are drawn together from a pack of 52 cards. The probability that one is a spade and one is a heart, is:}$$ A. $$3 \div 20$$ B. $$29 \div 34$$ C. $$47 \div 100$$ D. $$13 \div 102$$

答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{P.W.} = 	ext{Rs.}~\left(2562 - 122ight) = 	ext{Rs.}~	ext{2440}.$$
$$	ext{S.I. on Rs.}~	ext{2440 for 4 months is Rs.}~	ext{122}.$$
$$	ext{Rate} =  rac{100 	imes 122}{2440 	imes  rac{1}{3}} = 15\%.$$

question: $$	ext{A trader owes a merchant Rs. 10,028 due 1 year hence. The trader wants to settle the account after 3 months. If the rate of interest 12% per annum, how much cash should he pay?}$$ A. $$Rs. 9025.20$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 9200}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 9600}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 9560}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Required money} = 	ext{P.W. of Rs. 10028 due 9 months hence}$$

$$= 	ext{Rs.} \left[  rac{10028 	imes 100}{100 + \left(12 	imes  rac{9}{12}ight)} ight] = 	ext{Rs. 9200}.$$

question: $$	ext{A man wants to sell his scooter. There are two offers, one at Rs. 12,000 cash and the other a credit of Rs. 12,880 to be paid after 8 months, money being at 18% per annum. Which is the better offer?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 12,000 in cash}$$ B. $$	ext{s. 12,880 at credit}$$ C. $$	ext{Both are equally good}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$P.W. 	ext{ of Rs. } 12880 	ext{ due 8 months hence} =  rac{12880 	imes 100}{100 + \left(18 	imes  rac{8}{12}ight)} =  rac{12880 	imes 100}{112} = Rs. 11500.$$

question: $$	ext{If Rs. 10 be allowed as true discount on a bill of Rs. 110 due at the end of a certain time, then the discount allowed on the same sum due at the end of double the time is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 20}$$ B. $$Rs. 21.81$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 22}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 18.33}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{S.I. on Rs. (110 - 10) for a certain time = Rs. 10}.$$
$$	ext{S.I. on Rs. 100 for double the time = Rs. 20}.$$
$$	ext{T.D. on Rs. 120 = Rs. (120 - 100) = Rs. 20}.$$
$$	ext{T.D. on Rs. 110 = Rs. } \left(  rac{20 	imes 110}{120} ight) = 	ext{ Rs. 18.33}.$$

question: $$	ext{Goods were bought for Rs. 600 and sold the same for Rs. 688.50 at a credit of 9 months and thus gaining 2% The rate of interest per annum is:}$$ A. $$16 \div 2 	ext{%} =  rac{16}{2} 	ext{%} = 8 	ext{%}$$ B. $$14  rac{1}{2} \%$$ C. $$ rac{13}{3}\%$$ D. $$	ext{15\%}$$
answer:
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{S.P. = 102% of Rs. 600 = } 102 	imes 600 = 	ext{Rs. 612}.$$

$$	ext{P.W. = Rs. 612 and sum = Rs. 688.50}.$$

$$	ext{T.D. = Rs. (688.50 - 612) = Rs. 76.50}.$$

$$	ext{Thus, S.I. on Rs. 612 for 9 months is Rs. 76.50}.$$

$$	ext{Rate = }  rac{100 	imes 76.50}{612 	imes  rac{3}{4}} = 16 rac{2}{3}\%.$$

question: $$	ext{The true discount on a bill due 9 months hence at 16% per annum is Rs. 189. The amount of the bill is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 1386}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 1764}$$ C. $$Rs. 1575$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 2268}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Let P.W. be Rs. }x.$$
$$	ext{Then, S.I. on Rs. }x	ext{ at 16% for 9 months = Rs. 189}.$$
$$ rac{x \cdot 16 \cdot  rac{9}{12}}{100} = 189 \quad 	ext{or} \quad x = 1575.$$
$$	ext{P.W. = Rs. 1575}.$$
$$	ext{Sum due = P.W. + T.D. = Rs. (1575 + 189) = Rs. 1764}.$$

question: $$	ext{8. A man buys a watch for Rs. 1950 in cash and sells it for Rs. 2200 at a credit of 1 year. If the rate of interest is 10% per annum, the man:}$$ A. $$	ext{gains Rs. 55}$$ B. $$	ext{gains Rs. 50}$$ C. $$	ext{loses Rs. 30}$$ D. $$	ext{gains Rs. 30}$$

$$= 	ext{Rs. 2000}$$

$$	ext{Gain} = 	ext{Rs. (2000 - 1950)} = 	ext{Rs. 50}$$

question: $$	ext{The true discount on Rs. 1760 due after a certain time at 12% per annum is Rs. 160. The time after which it is due is:}$$ A. $$	ext{6 months}$$ B. $$	ext{8 months}$$ C. $$	ext{9 months}$$ D. $$	ext{10 months}$$

question: $$	ext{10. The present worth of Rs. 2310 due 2 rac{1}{2} years hence, the rate of interest being 15\% per annum, is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 1750}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 1680}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 1840}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 1443.75}$$

question: $$	ext{11. Rs. 20 is the true discount on Rs. 260 due after a certain time. What will be the true discount on the same sum due after half of the former time, the rate of interest being the same?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 10}$$ B. $$Rs. 10.40$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 15.20}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 13}$$
answer:

$$	ext{S.I. on Rs. 240 for half the time = Rs. 10}.$$
$$	ext{T.D. on Rs. 250 = Rs. 10}.$$

question: $$	ext{12. The interest on Rs. 750 for 2 years is the same as the true discount on Rs. 960 due 2 years hence. If the rate of interest is the same in both cases, it is:}$$ A. $$	ext{12\%}$$ B. $$	ext{14\%}$$ C. $$	ext{15\%}$$ D. $$ rac{16}{3} 	ext{ } (\%)$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{S.I. on Rs. 750 = T.D. on Rs. 960}.$$
$$	ext{This means P.W. of Rs. 960 due 2 years hence is Rs. 750}.$$
$$	ext{T.D. = Rs. (960 - 750) = Rs. 210}.$$
$$	ext{Thus, S.I. on R.s 750 for 2 years is Rs. 210}.$$
$$	ext{Rate =}  rac{100 	imes 210}{750 	imes 2} 	ext{ = 14\%}.$$

question: $$	ext{13. The simple interest and the true discount on a certain sum for a given time and at a given rate are Rs. 85 and Rs. 80 respectively. The sum is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 1800}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 1450}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 1360}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 6800}$$

question: $$	ext{14. The present worth of Rs. 1404 due in two equal half-yearly installments at 8% per annum simple interest is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 1325}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 1300}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 1350}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 1500}$$

$$= 	ext{Rs. (675 + 650)}$$

$$= 	ext{Rs. 1325}.$$

question: $$	ext{15. If the true discount on a sum due 2 years hence at 14\% per annum be Rs. 168, the sum due is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 768}$$ B. $$Rs. 968$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 1960}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 2400}$$

question: $$	ext{The banker's discount on a bill due 4 months hence at 15% is Rs. 420. The true discount is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 400}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 360}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 480}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 320}$$

question: $$	ext{The banker's discount on Rs. 1600 at 15% per annum is the same as true discount on Rs. 1680 for the same time and at the same rate. The time is:}$$ A. $$	ext{3 months}$$ B. $$	ext{4 months}$$ C. $$	ext{6 months}$$ D. $$	ext{8 months}$$

question: $$	ext{The banker's gain of a certain sum due 2 years hence at 10% per annum is Rs. 24. The present worth is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 480}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 520}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 600}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 960}$$

question: $$	ext{The banker's discount on a sum of money for 1 rac{1}{2} years is Rs. 558 and the true discount on the same sum for 2 years is Rs. 600. The rate percent is:}$$ A. $$	ext{10\%}$$ B. $$	ext{13\%}$$ C. $$	ext{12\%}$$ D. $$	ext{15\%}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{B.D. for }  rac{1}{3} 	ext{ years} = 	ext{Rs. 558}.$$

$$	ext{Thus, Rs. 744 is S.I. on Rs. 3100 for 2 years}.$$
$$	ext{Rate} =  rac{100 	imes 744}{3100 	imes 2} = 12\%.$$

question: $$	ext{The banker's discount of a certain sum of money is Rs. 72 and the true discount on the same sum for the same time is Rs. 60. The sum due is:}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 360}$$ B. $$Rs. 432$$ C. $$Rs. 540$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 1080}$$

$$

question: $$	ext{Total sales of branches B1, B3 and B5 together for both the years (in thousand numbers)?}$$ A. $$	ext{250}$$ B. $$	ext{310}$$ C. $$	ext{435}$$ D. $$560$$

question: $$	ext{If for a certain quantity of books, the publisher has to pay Rs. 30,600 as printing cost, then what will be amount of royalty to be paid for these books?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 19,450}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 21,200}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 22,950}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 26,150}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Let the amount of Royalty to be paid for these books be Rs. } r.$$

$$	ext{Then, 20 : 15 = 30600 : } r \quad \Rightarrow \quad r = 	ext{Rs.}$$

$$ rac{30600 	imes 15}{20} = 	ext{Rs. 22,950}.$$

question: $$	ext{What is the central angle of the sector corresponding to the expenditure incurred on Royalty?}$$ A. $$	ext{15°}$$ B. $$	ext{24°}$$ C. $$	ext{54°}$$ D. $$	ext{48°}$$

答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Central angle corresponding to Royalty} = (15\% 	ext{ of } 360)^\circ$$
$$=  rac{15}{100} 	imes 360^\circ$$
$$= 54^\circ.$$

question: $$	ext{The price of the book is marked 20% above the C.P. If the marked price of the book is Rs. 180, then what is the cost of the paper used in a single copy of the book?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 36}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 37.50}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 42}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 44.25}$$
answer:
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, marked price of the book = 120\% of C.P.}$$
$$	ext{Also, cost of paper = 25\% of C.P}$$
$$	ext{Let the cost of paper for a single book be Rs. } n.$$

Then, 120 : 25 = 180 : \(n\)  ⇒  \(n\) = Rs. 
\[ rac{25 	imes 180}{120} = 	ext{Rs. 37.50}.\]

question: $$	ext{If 5500 copies are published and the transportation cost on them amounts to Rs. 82500, then what should be the selling price of the book so that the publisher can earn a profit of 25%?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 187.50}$$ B. $$Rs. 191.50$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 175}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 180}$$
answer:
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{For the publisher to earn a profit of 25%, S.P. = 125% of C.P.}$$
$$	ext{Also Transportation Cost = 10% of C.P.}$$
$$	ext{Let the S.P. of 5500 books be Rs. }x.$$

$$	ext{Then, 10 : 125 = 82500 : }x \quad \Rightarrow \quad x = 	ext{Rs.}$$
$$\left( rac{125 	imes 82500}{10}ight) = 	ext{Rs. 1031250}.$$

$$	ext{Therefore, S.P. of one book = Rs.}$$
$$\left( rac{1031250}{5500}ight) = 	ext{Rs. 187.50}.$$

question: $$	ext{Royalty on the book is less than the printing cost by:}$$ A. $$	ext{5\%}$$ B. $$ rac{33}{5} \%$$ C. $$	ext{20\%}$$ D. $$	ext{25\%}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Printing Cost of book} = 20\% 	ext{ of C.P.}$$
$$	ext{Royalty on book} = 15\% 	ext{ of C.P.}$$
$$	ext{Difference} = (20\% 	ext{ of C.P.}) - (15\% 	ext{ of C.P.}) = 5\% 	ext{ of C.P.}$$
$$	ext{Percentage difference} = \left(  rac{5\% 	ext{ of C.P.}}{20\% 	ext{ of C.P.}} ight) 	imes 100\% = 25\%.$$

question: $$	ext{If the difference between the two expenditures are represented by 18° in the pie-chart, then these expenditures possibly are}$$ A. $$	ext{Binding Cost and Promotion Cost}$$ B. $$	ext{Paper Cost and Royalty}$$ C. $$	ext{Binding Cost and Printing Cost}$$ D. $$	ext{Paper Cost and Printing Cost}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Central angle of 18°} =  rac{18}{360} 	imes 100 = 5\% 	ext{ of the total expenditure}.$$

$$	ext{Paper Cost - Printing Cost} = (25\% - 20\%) 	ext{ of the total expenditure} = 5\% 	ext{ of the total expenditure}.$$

question: $$	ext{7. For an edition of 12,500 copies, the amount of Royalty paid by the publisher is Rs. 2,81,250. What should be the selling price of the book if the publisher desires a profit of 5%?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 152.50}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 157.50}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 162.50}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 167.50}$$

$$	ext{Therefore, S.P. of one book = Rs. }  rac{1968750}{12500} = 	ext{Rs. } 157.50.$$

question: $$	ext{If for an edition of the book, the cost of paper is Rs. 56250, then find the promotion cost for this edition.}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 20,000}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 22,500}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 25,500}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 28,125}$$

question: $$	ext{Which two expenditures together have central angle of 108°?}$$ A. $$	ext{Binding Cost and Transportation Cost}$$ B. $$	ext{Printing Cost and Paper Cost}$$ C. $$	ext{Royalty and Promotion Cost}$$ D. $$	ext{Binding Cost and Paper Cost}$$
answer:
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Central angle of 108°} =  rac{108}{360} 	imes 100\% = 30\% 	ext{ of the total expenditure}.$$

$$	ext{Binding Cost + Transportation Cost} = (20\% + 10\%) 	ext{ of the total expenditure} = 30\% 	ext{ of the total expenditure}.$$

$$	ext{Therefore, Binding Cost and Transportation Cost together have a central angle of 108°}.$$

question: $$	ext{For which of the following pairs of years the total exports from the three Companies together are equal?}$$ A. $$	ext{1995 and 1998}$$ B. $$	ext{1996 and 1998}$$ C. $$	ext{1997 and 1998}$$ D. $$	ext{1995 and 1996}$$
answer:
答案是:D 解析如下: $$In 1993 = Rs. (30 + 80 + 60) crores = Rs. 170 crores.$$
$$In 1994 = Rs. (60 + 40 + 90) crores = Rs. 190 crores.$$
$$In 1995 = Rs. (40 + 60 + 120) crores = Rs. 220 crores.$$
$$In 1996 = Rs. (70 + 60 + 90) crores = Rs. 220 crores.$$
$$In 1997 = Rs. (100 + 80 + 60) crores = Rs. 240 crores.$$
$$In 1998 = Rs. (50 + 100 + 80) crores = Rs. 230 crores.$$
$$In 1999 = Rs. (120 + 140 + 100) crores = Rs. 360 crores.$$
$$Clearly, the total exports of the three Companies X, Y and Z together are same during the years 1995 and 1996.$$

question: $$	ext{In which year was the difference between the exports from Companies X and Y the minimum?}$$ A. $$	ext{1994}$$ B. $$	ext{1995}$$ C. $$	ext{1996}$$ D. $$1997$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$In\ 1993 = Rs.\ (80 - 30)\ crores = Rs.\ 50\ crores.$$
$$In\ 1994 = Rs.\ (60 - 40)\ crores = Rs.\ 20\ crores.$$
$$In\ 1995 = Rs.\ (60 - 40)\ crores = Rs.\ 20\ crores.$$
$$In\ 1996 = Rs.\ (70 - 60)\ crores = Rs.\ 10\ crores.$$
$$In\ 1997 = Rs.\ (100 - 80)\ crores = Rs.\ 20\ crores.$$
$$In\ 1998 = Rs.\ (100 - 50)\ crores = Rs.\ 50\ crores.$$
$$In\ 1999 = Rs.\ (140 - 120)\ crores = Rs.\ 20\ crores.$$
$$Clearly,\ the\ difference\ is\ minimum\ in\ the\ year\ 1996.$$

question: $$	ext{What was the difference between the average exports of the three Companies in 1993 and the average exports in 1998?}$$ A. $$	ext{Rs. 15.33 crores}$$ B. $$	ext{Rs. 18.67 crores}$$ C. $$	ext{Rs. 20 crores}$$ D. $$	ext{Rs. 22.17 crores}$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Average exports of the three Companies X, Y and Z in 1993} =  rac{1}{3} 	imes (30 + 80 + 60) 	ext{ crores} =  rac{170}{3} 	ext{ crores}.$$

$$	ext{Average exports of the three Companies X, Y and Z in 1998} =  rac{1}{3} 	imes (50 + 100 + 80) 	ext{ crores} =  rac{230}{3} 	ext{ crores}.$$

$$	ext{Difference} =  rac{1}{3} 	imes (230 - 170) 	ext{ crores} =  rac{60}{3} 	ext{ crores} = 20 	ext{ crores}.$$

question: $$	ext{In how many of the given years, were the exports from Company Z more than the average annual exports over the given years?}$$ A. $$2$$ B. $$3$$ C. $$4$$ D. $$5$$
answer:
答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Average annual exports of Company Z during the given period} =  rac{1}{7} 	imes (60 + 90 + 120 + 90 + 60 + 80 + 100)$$

$$= 	ext{Rs.}  rac{600}{7} 	ext{ crores} = 	ext{Rs. 85.71 crores}.$$

$$	ext{From the analysis of graph, the exports of Company Z are more than the average annual exports of Company Z (i.e., Rs. 85.71 crores) during the years 1994, 1995, 1996 and 1999, i.e., during 4 of the given years}.$$

question: $$	ext{Which word does NOT belong with the others?}$$ A. $$$$ B. $$	ext{trivial}$$ C. $$	ext{insignificant}$$ D. $$	ext{familiar}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{The first three choices are all synonyms.}$$

question: $$	ext{desert}$$ A. $$	ext{cactus}$$ B. $$	ext{arid}$$ C. $$	ext{oasis}$$ D. $$	ext{flat}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{A desert is an arid tract of land. Not all deserts are flat (choice d). Not all deserts have cacti or oases (choices a and c).}$$

question: $$	ext{book}$$ A. $$	ext{fiction}$$ B. $$	ext{pages}$$ C. $$	ext{pictures}$$ D. $$	ext{learning}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The necessary part of a book is its pages; there is no book without pages. Not all books are fiction (choice a), and not all books have pictures (choice c). Learning (choice d) may or may not take place with a book}$$

question: $$	ext{language}$$ A. $$	ext{tongue}$$ B. $$	ext{slang}$$ C. $$	ext{writing}$$ D. $$	ext{words}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Words are a necessary part of language. Slang is not necessary to language (choice b). Not all languages are written (choice c). Not all languages are spoken (choice a).}$$

question: $$	ext{school}$$ A. $$	ext{student}$$ B. $$	ext{report card}$$ C. $$	ext{test}$$ D. $$	ext{learning}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Without students, a school cannot exist; therefore, students are the essential part of schools. The other choices may be related, but they are not essential.}$$

question: $$	ext{monopoly}$$ A. $$	ext{corrupt}$$ B. $$	ext{exclusive}$$ C. $$	ext{rich}$$ D. $$	ext{gigantic}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The essential part of a monopoly is that it involves exclusive ownership or control.}$$

question: $$	ext{guitar}$$ A. $$	ext{band}$$ B. $$	ext{teacher}$$ C. $$	ext{songs}$$ D. $$	ext{strings}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{A guitar does not exist without strings, so strings are an essential part of a guitar. A band is not necessary to a guitar (choice a). Guitar playing can be learned without a teacher (choice b). Songs are byproducts of a guitar (choice c).}$$

question: $$	ext{13. lightning}$$ A. $$	ext{electricity}$$ B. $$	ext{thunder}$$ C. $$	ext{brightness}$$ D. $$	ext{rain}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Lightning is produced from a discharge of electricity, so electricity is essential. Thunder and rain are not essential to the production of lightning (choices b and d). Brightness may be a byproduct of lightning, but it is not essential (choice c).}$$

question: $$	ext{Odometer is to mileage as compass is to}$$ A. $$	ext{speed}$$ B. $$	ext{hiking}$$ C. $$	ext{needle}$$ D. $$	ext{direction}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{An odometer is an instrument used to measure mileage. A compass is an instrument used to determine direction. Choices a, b, and c are incorrect because none is an instrument.}$$

question: $$	ext{Marathon is to race as hibernation is to}$$ A. $$	ext{winter}$$ B. $$	ext{bear}$$ C. $$	ext{dream}$$ D. $$	ext{sleep}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{A marathon is a long race and hibernation is a lengthy period of sleep. The answer is not choice a or b because even though a bear and winter are related to hibernation, neither completes the analogy. (Choice c) is incorrect because sleep and dream are not synonymous.}$$

question: $$	ext{Window is to pane as book is to}$$ A. $$	ext{novel}$$ B. $$	ext{glass}$$ C. $$	ext{cover}$$ D. $$	ext{page}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{A window is made up of panes, and a book is made up of pages. The answer is not (choice a) because a novel is a type of book. The answer is not (choice b) because glass has no relationship to a book. (Choice c) is incorrect because a cover is only one part of a book; a book is not made up of covers.}$$

question: $$	ext{Cup is to coffee as bowl is to}$$ A. $$	ext{dish}$$ B. $$	ext{soup}$$ C. $$	ext{spoon}$$ D. $$	ext{food}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Coffee goes into a cup and soup goes into a bowl. Choices a and c are incorrect because they are other utensils. The answer is not choice d because the word food is too general.}$$

question: $$	ext{Yard is to inch as quart is to}$$ A. $$	ext{gallon}$$ B. $$	ext{ounce}$$ C. $$	ext{milk}$$ D. $$	ext{liquid}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{A yard is a larger measure than an inch (a yard contains 36 inches). A quart is a larger measure than an ounce (a quart contains 32 ounces). Gallon (choice a) is incorrect because it is larger than a quart. Choices c and d are incorrect because they are not units of measurement.}$$

question: $$	ext{Elated is to despondent as enlightened is to}$$ A. $$	ext{aware}$$ B. $$	ext{ignorant}$$ C. $$	ext{miserable}$$ D. $$	ext{tolerant}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Elated is the opposite of despondent; enlightened is the opposite of ignorant.}$$

question: $$	ext{Optimist is to cheerful as pessimist is to}$$ A. $$	ext{gloomy}$$ B. $$	ext{mean}$$ C. $$	ext{petty}$$ D. $$	ext{helpful}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{An optimist is a person whose outlook is cheerful. A pessimist is a person whose outlook is gloomy. The answer is not (choice b) because a pessimist does not have to be mean. (Choices c) and d are incorrect because neither adjective describes the outlook of a pessimist.}$$

question: $$	ext{Reptile is to lizard as flower is to}$$ A. $$	ext{petal}$$ B. $$	ext{stem}$$ C. $$	ext{daisy}$$ D. $$	ext{alligator}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{A lizard is a type of reptile; a daisy is a type of flower. Choices a and b are incorrect because a petal and a stem are parts of a flower, not types of flowers. (Choice d) is incorrect because an alligator is another type of reptile, not a type of flower.}$$

question: $$	ext{Play is to actor as concert is to}$$ A. $$	ext{symphony}$$ B. $$	ext{musician}$$ C. $$	ext{piano}$$ D. $$	ext{percussion}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{An actor performs in a play. A musician performs at a concert. Choices a, c, and d are incorrect because none is people who perform.}$$

question: $$	ext{Sponge is to porous as rubber is to}$$ A. $$	ext{massive}$$ B. $$	ext{solid}$$ C. $$	ext{elastic}$$ D. $$	ext{in}
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{A sponge is a porous material. Rubber is an elastic material. (Choice a) is incorrect because rubber would not generally be referred to as massive. The answer is not (choice b) because even though rubber is a solid, its most noticeable characteristic is its elasticity. Choice d is incorrect because rubber has flexibility.}$$

question: $$	ext{11. Careful is to cautious as boastful is to}$$ A. $$	ext{arrogant}$$ B. $$	ext{humble}$$ C. $$	ext{joyful}$$ D. $$	ext{suspicious}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Careful and cautious are synonyms (they mean the same thing). Boastful and arrogant are also synonyms. The answer is not (choice b) because humble means the opposite of boastful. The answer is not choice c or d because neither means the same as boastful.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Pen is to poet as needle is to}$$ A. $$	ext{thread}$$ B. ```tex
$$	ext{button}$$
``` C. $$	ext{sewing}$$ D. $$	ext{tailor}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{A pen is a tool used by a poet. A needle is a tool used by a tailor. The answer is not choice a, b, or c because none is a person and therefore cannot complete the analogy.}$$

question: $$	ext{Secretly is to openly as silently is to}$$ A. $$	ext{scarcely}$$ B. $$	ext{impolitely}$$ C. $$	ext{noisily}$$ D. $$	ext{quietly}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Secretly is the opposite of openly, and silently is the opposite of noisily. Choices a and b are clearly not the opposites of silently. (Choice d) means the same thing as silently.}$$

question: $$	ext{Embarrassed is to humiliated as frightened is to}$$ A. $$	ext{terrified}$$ B. $$	ext{agitated}$$ C. $$	ext{courageous}$$ D. $$	ext{reckless}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{If someone has been humiliated, they have been greatly embarrassed. If someone is terrified, they are extremely frightened. The answer is not choice b because an agitated person is not necessarily frightened. Choices c and d are incorrect because neither word expresses a state of being frightened.}$$

question: $$	ext{Pride is to lion as shoal is to}$$ A. $$	ext{teacher}$$ B. $$	ext{student}$$ C. $$	ext{self-respect}$$ D. $$	ext{fish}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{A group of lions is called a pride. A group of fish swim in a shoal. Teacher (choice a) and student (choice b) refer to another meaning of the word school. The answer is not (choice c) because self-respect has no obvious relationship to this particular meaning of school.}$$

question: $$	ext{Artist is to painting as senator is to}$$ A. $$	ext{attorney}$$ B. $$	ext{law}$$ C. $$	ext{politician}$$ D. $$	ext{constituents}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{An artist makes paintings; a senator makes laws. The answer is not choice a because an attorney does not make laws and a senator is not an attorney. Choice c is incorrect because a senator is not a politician. Constituents (choice d) is also incorrect because a senator serves his or her constituents.}$$

question: $$	ext{Exercise is to gym as eating is to}$$ A. $$	ext{food}$$ B. $$	ext{dieting}$$ C. $$	ext{fitness}$$ D. $$	ext{restaurant}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{A gym is a place where people exercise. A restaurant is a place where people eat. Food (choice a) is not the answer because it is something people eat, not a place or location where they eat. The answer is not choice b or c because neither represents a place where people eat.}$$

question: $$	ext{Candid is to indirect as honest is to}$$ A. $$	ext{frank}$$ B. $$	ext{wicked}$$ C. $$	ext{truthful}$$ D. $$	ext{untruthful}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Candid and indirect refer to opposing traits. Honest and untruthful refer to opposing traits. The answer is not choice a because frank means the same thing as candid. Wicked (choice b) is incorrect because even though it refers to a negative trait, it does not mean the opposite of honest. (Choice c) is incorrect because truthful and honest mean the same thing.}$$

question: $$	ext{Guide is to direct as reduce is to}$$ A. $$	ext{decrease}$$ B. $$	ext{maintain}$$ C. $$	ext{increase}$$ D. $$	ext{preserve}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Guide and direct are synonyms, and reduce and decrease are synonyms. The answer is not choice b or d because neither means the same as reduce. (Choice c) is incorrect because increase is the opposite of reduce.}$$

question: $$	ext{Oar is to rowboat as foot is to}$$ A. $$	ext{running}$$ B. $$	ext{sneaker}$$ C. $$	ext{skateboard}$$ D. $$	ext{jumping}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{An oar puts a rowboat into motion. A foot puts a skateboard into motion. The answer is not choice a because running is not an object that is put into motion by a foot. Sneaker (choice b) is incorrect because it is something worn on a foot. Jumping (choice d) is incorrect because although you do need feet to jump, jumping is not an object that is put into motion by means of a foot.}$$

question:
$$	ext{1.}$$
$$	ext{Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}$$
$$	ext{gorblflur means fan belt}$$
$$	ext{pixngorbl means ceiling fan}$$
$$	ext{arthtusl means tile roof}$$
$$	ext{Which word could mean "ceiling tile"?}$$ A. $$	ext{gorbltusl}$$ B. $$	ext{flurgorbl}$$ C. $$	ext{arthflur}$$ D. $$	ext{pixnarth}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Gorbl means fan; flur means belt; pixn means ceiling; arth means tile; and tusl means roof. Therefore, pixnarth is the correct choice.}$$

question:
$$	ext{2. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{hapllesh} means cloudburst.}~\
	ext{	extit{srenchoch} means pinball.}~\
	ext{	extit{resbosrench} means ninepin.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "cloud nine"}?$$ A. $$	ext{leshsrench}$$ B. $$	ext{ochhapl}$$ C. $$	ext{haploch}$$ D. $$	ext{haplresbo}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Hapl means cloud; lesh means burst; srench means pin; och means ball; and resbo means nine. Leshsrench (choice a) doesn't contain any of the words needed for cloud nine. We know that och means ball, so that rules out choices b and c. When you combine hapl (cloud) with resbo (nine), you get the correct answer}$$

question:
$$	ext{3.}$$
$$	ext{Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}$$
$$	ext{agnoscrenia means poisonous spider}$$
$$	ext{delanocrenia means poisonous snake}$$
$$	ext{agnosdeery means brown spider}$$
$$	ext{Which word could mean "black widow spider"?}$$ A. $$	ext{deeryclostagnos}$$ B. $$	ext{agnosdelano}$$ C. $$	ext{agnosvitriblunin}$$ D. $$	ext{trymuttiagnos}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{In this language, the noun appears first and the adjectives follow. Since } 	ext{agnos} 	ext{ means spider and should appear first, choices a and d can be ruled out. Choice b can be ruled out because } 	ext{delano} 	ext{ means snake.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{moolokarn}} 	ext{ means blue sky.}~\
	ext{	extit{wilkospadi}} 	ext{ means bicycle race.}~\
	ext{	extit{moolowilko}} 	ext{ means blue bicycle.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "racecar"}?$$ A. $$	ext{wilkozwet}$$ B. $$	ext{spadiwilko}$$ C. $$	ext{moolobreil}$$ D. $$	ext{spadivolo}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{From 	extit{wilkospadi}, you can determine that 	extit{wilko} means bicycle and 	extit{spadi} means race. Therefore, the first part of the word that means racecar should begin with 	extit{spadi}. That limits your choices to b and d. Choice b, 	extit{spadiwilko}, is incorrect because we have already determined that wilko means bicycle. Therefore, the answer must be choice d, 	extit{spadivolo}.}$$

question:
$$	ext{5. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{migenlasan} means cupboard.}~\
	ext{	extit{lasanpoen} means boardwalk.}~\
	ext{	extit{cuopdansa} means pullman.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "walkway"?}$$ A. $$	ext{poenmigen}$$ B. $$	ext{cuopeisel}$$ C. $$	ext{lasandansa}$$ D. $$	ext{poenforc}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Migen means cup; lasan means board; poen means walk; cuop means pull; and dansa means man. The only possible choices, then, are choices a and d. Choice a can be ruled out because migen means cup.}$$

question:
$$	ext{6. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{godabim} means kidney stones.}~\
	ext{	extit{romzbim} means kidney beans.}~\
	ext{	extit{romzbako} means wax beans.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "wax statue"}?$$ A. $$	ext{godaromz}$$ B. $$	ext{lazbim}$$ C. $$	ext{wasibako}$$ D. $$	ext{romzpeo}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{In this language, the adjective follows the noun. From } 	ext{godabim} 	ext{ and } 	ext{romzbim}, 	ext{ you can determine that bim means kidney. From } 	ext{romzbim} 	ext{ and } 	ext{romzbako}, 	ext{ you can determine that } 	ext{romz} 	ext{ means beans. Therefore, } 	ext{bako} 	ext{ means wax. Because the adjective wax must come after the noun in this language, } 	ext{wasibako} 	ext{ is the only choice.}$$

question:
$$	ext{7. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{granamelke} means big tree.}~\
	ext{	extit{pinimelke} means little tree.}~\
	ext{	extit{melkehoon} means tree house.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "big house"}?$$ A. $$	ext{granahoon}$$ B. $$	ext{pinishur}$$ C. $$	ext{pinihoon}$$ D. $$	ext{melkegrana}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Grana means big; melke means tree; pini means little; hoon means house. Therefore, granahoon means big house.}$$

question:
$$	ext{8. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{daftafoni} means advisement.}~\
	ext{	extit{imodafta} means misadvise.}~\
	ext{	extit{imolokti} means misconduct.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "statement"}?$$ A. $$	ext{kratafoni}$$ B. $$	ext{kratadafta}$$ C. $$	ext{loktifoni}$$ D. $$	ext{daftaimo}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Dafta means advise; foni is the same as the suffix -ment; imo is the same as the prefix mis-; lokti means conduct. Since the only word in the answer choices that hasn't been defined is krata, it is reasonable to assume that krata means state. Therefore, kratafoni is the only choice that could mean statement.}$$

question:
$$	ext{9. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{lelibroon}} 	ext{ means yellow hat.}~\
	ext{	extit{plekafroti}} 	ext{ means flower garden.}~\
	ext{	extit{frotimix}} 	ext{ means garden salad.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "yellow flower"}?$$ A. $$	ext{lelifroti}$$ B. $$	ext{lelipleka}$$ C. $$	ext{plekabroon}$$ D. $$	ext{frotibroon}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Leli means yellow; broon means hat; pleka means flower; froti means garden; mix means salad. Therefore, lelipleka means yellow flower.}$$

question:
$$	ext{10. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{myncabel} means saddle horse.}~\
	ext{	extit{conowir} means trail ride.}~\
	ext{	extit{cabelalma} means horse blanket.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "horse ride"?}$$ A. $$	ext{cabelwir}$$ B. $$	ext{conocabel}$$ C. $$	ext{almamyn}$$ D. $$	ext{conoalma}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Myn means saddle; cabel means horse; cono means trail; and wir means ride. Therefore, cabelwir is the correct answer.}$$

question:
$$	ext{11. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{dionot} means oak tree.}~\
	ext{	extit{blyonot} means oak leaf.}~\
	ext{	extit{blycrin} means maple leaf.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "maple syrup"}?$$ A. $$	ext{blymuth}$$ B. $$	ext{hupponot}$$ C. $$	ext{patricrin}$$ D. $$	ext{crinweel}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{In this language, the adjective follows the noun. From 	extit{dionot} and 	extit{blyonot}, you can determine that 'onot' means oak. From 	extit{blyonot} and 	extit{blycrin}, you can determine that 	extit{bly} means leaf. Therefore, 	extit{crin} means maple. Because the adjective maple comes after the noun, 	extit{patricrin} is the only possible choice.}$$

question:
$$	ext{12. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.}~\
	ext{	extit{tamceno} means sky blue.}~\
	ext{	extit{cenorax} means blue cheese.}~\
	ext{	extit{aplmitl} means star bright.}~\
	ext{Which word could mean "bright sky"?}$$ A. $$	ext{cenotam}$$ B. $$	ext{mitltam}$$ C. $$	ext{raxmitl}$$ D. $$	ext{aplceno}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Tam means sky; ceno means blue; rax means cheese; apl means star; and mitl means bright. So, mitltam means bright sky.}$$

question: $$	ext{Applying for Seasonal Employment}$$ occurs when a person requests to be considered for a job that is dependent on a particular season or time of year. Which situation below is the best example of $$	ext{Applying for Seasonal Employment}$$? A. $$	ext{The ski instructors at Top of the Peak Ski School work from December through March.}$$ B. $$	ext{Matthew prefers jobs that allow him to work outdoors.}$$ C. $$	ext{Lucinda makes an appointment with the beach resort restaurant manager to interview for the summer waitressing position that was advertised in the newspaper.}$$ D. $$	ext{Doug's ice cream shop stays open until 11 p.m. during the summer months.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Although the ski instructors at Top of the Peak Ski School do work seasonally, choice a does not describe anyone applying for seasonal employment. In choice b, the statement that Matthew likes to work outdoors tells us nothing about seasonal employment or someone applying for it. And although choice d describes a business with seasonal hours, it does not describe a person applying for seasonal work. Choice c, on the other hand, very specifically depicts a person, Lucinda, who is applying for a job as a summer waitress at a beach resort, which is dependent upon a particular season of the year.}$$

question: $$	ext{Violating an Apartment Lease occurs when a tenant does something prohibited by the legally binding document that he or she has signed with a landlord. Which situation below is the best example of Violating an Apartment Lease?}$$ A. $$	ext{Tim has decided to move to another city, so he calls his landlord to tell him that he is not interested in renewing his lease when it expires next month.}$$ B. $$	ext{Valerie recently lost her job and, for the last three months, has neglected to pay her landlord the monthly rent they agreed upon in writing when she moved into her apartment eight months ago.}$$ C. $$	ext{Mark writes a letter to his landlord that lists numerous complaints about the apartment he has agreed to rent for two years.}$$ D. $$	ext{Leslie thinks that her landlord is neglecting the building in which she rents an apartment. She calls her attorney to ask for advice.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Valerie signed a legally binding document that requires her to pay a monthly rent for her apartment and she has failed to do this for the last three months. Therefore, she has violated her apartment lease.}$$

question: $$	ext{An Informal Gathering occurs when a group of people get together in a casual, relaxed manner. Which situation below is the best example of an Informal Gathering?}$$ A. $$	ext{The book club meets on the first Thursday evening of every month.}$$ B. $$	ext{After finding out about his promotion, Jeremy and a few coworkers decide to go out for a quick drink after work.}$$ C. $$	ext{Mary sends out 25 invitations for the bridal shower she is giving for her sister.}$$ D. $$	ext{Whenever she eats at the Mexican restaurant, Clara seems to run into Peter.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{After getting some good news, Jeremy and a few friends casually get together for a drink after work, thereby having an informal gathering. Choices a and c describe more formal types of gatherings. Choice d describes a chance or coincidental kind of meeting.}$$

question: $$	ext{People 	extbf{speculate} when they consider a situation and assume something to be true based on inconclusive evidence. Which situation below is the best example of Speculation ?}$$ A. $$	ext{Francine decides that it would be appropriate to wear jeans to her new office on Friday after reading about "Casual Fridays" in her employee handbook.}$$ B. $$	ext{Mary spends thirty minutes sitting in traffic and wishes that she took the train instead of driving.}$$ C. $$	ext{After consulting several guidebooks and her travel agent, Jennifer feels confident that the hotel she has chosen is first-rate.}$$ D. $$	ext{When Emily opens the door in tears, Theo guesses that she's had a death in her family.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{This is the only situation in which someone makes an assumption that is not based on conclusive evidence. Choices a and c reflect situations in which assumptions are made based on evidence. In choice b, Mary is not assuming anything to be true. She is simply wishing that she'd made a different decision.}$$

question: $$	ext{Posthumous Publication occurs when a book is published after the author's death. Which situation below is the best example of Posthumous Publication?}$$ A. $$	ext{Richard's illness took his life before he was able to enjoy the amazing early reviews of his novel.}$$ B. $$	ext{Melissa's publisher cancels her book contract after she fails to deliver the manuscript on time.}$$ C. $$	ext{Clarence never thought he'd live to see the third book in his trilogy published.}$$ D. $$	ext{Elizabeth is honored with a prestigious literary award for her writing career and her daughter accepts the award on behalf of her deceased mother.}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Although choice d also mentions a writer who has died, it does not state that one of the writer's books was published after her death, only that she received an award. Choice a states that Richard wasn't around to see the early reviews of his novel, therefore implying that Richard died before the book was published. The other two options depict living writers.}$$

question: $$	ext{6. A 	extbf{Guarantee} is a promise or assurance that attests to the quality of a product that is either (1) given in writing by the manufacturer or (2) given verbally by the person selling the product. Which situation below is the best example of a Guarantee?}$$ A. $$	ext{Melissa purchases a DVD player with the highest consumer ratings in its category.}$$ B. $$	ext{The salesperson advises Curt to be sure that he buys an air conditioner with a guarantee.}$$ C. $$	ext{The local auto body shop specializes in refurbishing and selling used cars.}$$ D. $$	ext{Lori buys a used digital camera from her coworker who says that she will refund Lori's money if the camera's performance is not of the highest quality.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Choices a, b, and c do not describe situations in which a product is guaranteed. Only choice d reflects a situation in which a seller attests to the quality of a product by giving the buyer a promise or assurance about its quality.}$$

question: $$	ext{7. The rules of baseball state that a batter 	extbf{Legally Completes His Time at Bat} when he is put out or becomes a base runner. Which situation below is the best example of a batter Legally Completing His Time at Bat?}$$ A. $$	ext{Jared's blooper over the head of the short-stop puts him in scoring position.}$$ B. $$	ext{The umpire calls a strike, even though the last pitch was way outside.}$$ C. $$	ext{The pitcher throws his famous knuckleball, Joe swings and misses, and the umpire calls a strike.}$$ D. $$	ext{The count is two balls and two strikes as Mario waits for the next pitch.}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The fact that Jared is in scoring position due to his blooper indicates that he has hit the ball and is now a base runner; therefore, he has legally completed his time at bat. Choices b and c both describe situations in which a strike is called, but they do not state that the batter has been put out or that he is now a base runner. Choice d describes a situation in which the batter, Mario, is still at the plate waiting for the next pitch.}$$

question: $$	ext{Erratic Behavior occurs when an individual acts in a manner that lacks consistency, regularity, and uniformity. Which situation below is the best example of Erratic Behavior?}$$ A. $$	ext{Julia cannot contain her anger whenever the subject of local politics is discussed.}$$ B. $$	ext{Martin has just been told that he is being laid off. Before leaving his supervisor's office, he punches a hole in the door.}$$ C. $$	ext{Rhonda has visited the dealership several times, but she still cannot decide which car to buy.}$$ D. $$	ext{In the past month, Jeffrey, who has been a model employee for three years, has repeatedly called in sick, forgotten important meetings, and been verbally abusive to colleagues.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Jeffrey's recent behavior is clearly inconsistent and irregular.}$$

question: $$	ext{9. A Tiebreaker is an additional contest or period of play designed to establish a winner among tied contestants. Which situation below is the best example of a Tiebreaker?}$$ A. $$	ext{At halftime, the score is tied at 28.}$$ B. $$	ext{Mary and Megan have each scored three goals in the game.}$$ C. $$	ext{The referee tosses a coin to decide which team will have possession of the ball first.}$$ D. $$	ext{The Sharks and the Bears each finished with 14 points, and they are now battling it out in a five-minute overtime.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{This is the only choice that indicates that an additional period of play is taking place to determine the winner of a game that ended in a tie.}$$

question: $$	ext{10. In the Maple Hill school district, a Five-Day Suspension occurs when a student is not permitted to attend school for five days for (1) physically assaulting another student, a teacher, or a school employee or (2) willfully destructing or defacing school property. Which situation below is the best example of a Five-Day Suspension?}$$ A. $$	ext{Lillian gets caught cheating on a math test for the second time and is suspended from school.}$$ B. $$	ext{Marc is asked to leave the classroom due to his constant disruptions.}$$ C. $$	ext{Franny uses spray paint to write derogatory comments on the locker room wall and she is given a suspension.}$$ D. $$	ext{Ms. Farmer tells her class that students who fail the midterm exam will be expected to stay after school for tutoring help.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Although choices a and c both describe suspensions, only choice c describes a suspension that is the result of one of the two scenarios given in the definition of a five-day suspension (physical assault or destructing or defacing school property). Therefore, we can assume that Franny's suspension, which is the result of spray painting school property, will be a five-day suspension. Since the definition doesn't provide any information about suspensions for cheating, we can assume that Lillian's suspension does not fall into the five-day suspension category.}$$

question: $$	ext{It is appropriate to compensate someone if you have damaged his or her property in some way. This is called 	extbf{Restitution}. Which situation below is the best example of Restitution?}$$ A. $$	ext{Jake borrows Leslie's camera and the lens shatters when it falls on the ground because he fails to zipper the case. When Jake returns the camera, he tells Leslie that he will pay for the repair.}$$ B. $$	ext{Rebecca borrows her neighbor's car, and when she returns it, the gas tank is practically empty. She apologizes profusely and tells her neighbor she will be more considerate the next time.}$$ C. $$	ext{Aaron asks Tom to check in on his apartment while he is out of town. When Tom arrives, he discovers that a pipe has burst and there is a considerable amount of water damage. He calls a plumber to repair the pipe.}$$ D. $$	ext{Lisa suspects that the pothole in her company's parking lot caused her flat tire. She tells her boss that she thinks the company should pay for the repair.}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Jake damaged Leslie's camera while it was in his possession and he has agreed to compensate Leslie for the cost of the repair.}$$

question: $$	ext{Reentry occurs when a person leaves his or her social system for a period of time and then returns. Which situation below best describes Reentry ?}$$ A. $$	ext{When he is offered a better paying position, Jacob leaves the restaurant he manages to manage a new restaurant on the other side of town.}$$ B. $$	ext{Catherine is spending her junior year of college studying abroad in France.}$$ C. $$	ext{Malcolm is readjusting to civilian life after two years of overseas military service.}$$ D. $$	ext{After several miserable months, Sharon decides that she can no longer share an apartment with her roommate Hilary.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Malcolm is the only person returning to a social system that he has been away from for an extended period of time.}$$

question: $$	ext{Embellishing the Truth occurs when a person adds fictitious details or exaggerates facts or true stories. Which situation below is the best example of Embellishing the Truth?}$$ A. $$	ext{Isabel goes to the theater, and the next day, she tells her coworkers she thought the play was excellent.}$$ B. $$	ext{The realtor describes the house, which is eleven blocks away from the ocean, as prime waterfront property.}$$ C. $$	ext{During the job interview, Fred, who has been teaching elementary school for ten years, describes himself as a very experienced teacher.}$$ D. $$	ext{The basketball coach says it is likely that only the most talented players will get a college scholarship.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The realtor is using a clear exaggeration when she states that a house which is eleven blocks away from the ocean is prime waterfront property.}$$

question: $$	ext{Establishing a Power of Attorney occurs when a legal document is created that gives one individual the authority to act for another. Which situation below is the best example of Establishing a Power of Attorney?}$$ A. $$	ext{Louise is selling her house and she hires a lawyer to review the contract.}$$ B. $$	ext{Simone's mother can no longer get to the bank to cash her checks and make deposits, so she has taken legal steps to enable Simone to do these things for her.}$$ C. $$	ext{Jack's father is elderly and Jack thinks he is no longer able to make decisions for himself.}$$ D. $$	ext{At her daughter's urging, Mrs.Lenox opens up a retirement account with the local bank.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Simone's mother has taken legal steps to allow another person to act on her behalf. Therefore, this is the only choice that indicates that a power of attorney has been established.}$$

question: $$	ext{Eileen is planning a special birthday dinner for her husband's 35th birthday. She wants the evening to be memorable, but her husband is a simple man who would rather be in jeans at a baseball game than in a suit at a fancy restaurant. Which restaurant below should Eileen choose?}$$ A. $$	ext{Alfredo's offers fine Italian cuisine and an elegant Tuscan decor. Patrons will feel as though they've spent the evening in a luxurious Italian villa.}$$ B. $$	ext{Pancho's Mexican Buffet is an all-you-can-eat family style smorgasbord with the best tacos in town.}$$ C. $$	ext{The Parisian Bistro is a four-star French restaurant where guests are treated like royalty. Chef Dilbert Olay is famous for his beef bourguignon.}$$ D. $$	ext{Marty's serves delicious, hearty meals in a charming setting reminiscent of a baseball clubhouse in honor of the owner, Marty Lester, a former major league baseball all-star.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Since Eileen's husband does not enjoy fancy restaurants, choices a and c can be ruled out. Choice b, although casual, doesn't sound as though it would be the kind of special and memorable evening that Eileen is looking for. Choice d, which is owned by a former baseball star and is described as "charming" and "reminiscent of a baseball clubhouse", sounds perfect for Eileen's husband, who is described as a baseball fan and a man with simple tastes.}$$

question:
$$	ext{The film director wants an actress for the lead role of Lucy who perfectly fits the description that appears in the original screenplay. He is not willing to consider actresses who do not resemble the character as she is described in the screenplay, no matter how talented they are. The screenplay describes Lucy as an average-sized, forty something redhead, with deep brown eyes, very fair skin, and a brilliant smile. The casting agent has four actresses in mind.}$$

$$	ext{Actress \#1 is a stunning red-haired beauty who is 5'9" and in her mid-twenties. Her eyes are brown and she has an olive complexion.}$$

$$	ext{Actress \#2 has red hair, big brown eyes, and a fair complexion. She is in her mid-forties and is 5'5".}$$

$$	ext{Actress \#3 is 5'4" and of medium build. She has red hair, brown eyes, and is in her early forties.}$$

$$	ext{Actress \#4 is a blue-eyed redhead in her early thirties. She's of very slight build and stands at 5'.} A. $$	ext{1, 2}$$ B. $$2, 3$$ C. $$	ext{1, 4}$$ D. $$2, 4$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Actresses #2 and #3 possess most of the required traits. They both have red hair and brown eyes, are average-sized, and are in their forties. Actress #1 is very tall and is only in her mid-twenties. She also has an olive complexion. Actress #4 is of very slight build and is in her early thirties. She also has blue eyes.}$$

question: $$	ext{The school principal has received complaints from parents about bullying in the school yard during recess. He wants to investigate and end this situation as soon as possible, so he has asked the recess aides to watch closely. Which situation should the recess aides report to the principal?}$$ A. $$	ext{A girl is sitting glumly on a bench reading a book and not interacting with her peers.}$$ B. $$	ext{Four girls are surrounding another girl and seem to have possession of her backpack.}$$ C. $$	ext{Two boys are playing a one-on-one game of basketball and are arguing over the last basket scored.}$$ D. $$	ext{Three boys are huddled over a handheld video game, which isn't supposed to be on school grounds.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Seeing four girls surrounding another girl, while in possession of her backpack, is the most suspicious of the incidents described.}$$

question:
$$	ext{4. Mrs. Carson took a taxi to meet her three friends for lunch. They were waiting for her outside the restaurant when she pulled up in the car. She was so excited to see her friends that she left her tote bag in the taxi. As the taxi pulled away, she and her friends took notice of the license plate number so they would be able to identify the car when they called the taxi company.} $$
$$	ext{	extbf{#1}: The four women seem to agree that the plate starts out with the letter J.}$$
$$	ext{	extbf{#2}: Three of them agree that the plate ends with 12L.}$$
$$	ext{	extbf{#3}: Three of them think that the second letter is X, and a different three think that the third letter is K.}$$
$$	ext{The four license plate numbers below represent what each of the four women thinks she saw. Which one is most likely the license plate number of the taxi?}$$ A. $$JXK 12L$$ B. $$	ext{JYK 12L}$$ C. $$	ext{JXK 12I}$$ D. $$	ext{JXX 12L}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The four women seem to agree that the plate starts out with the letter J. Three of them agree that the plate ends with 12L. Three of them think that the second letter is X, and a different three think that the third letter is K. The plate description that has all of these common elements is "Option A".}$$

question:
$$	ext{5. Zachary has invited his three buddies over to watch the basketball game on his wide-screen television. They are all hungry, but no one wants to leave to get food. Just as they are arguing about who should make the food run, a commercial comes on for a local pizzeria that delivers. The phone number flashes on the screen briefly and they all try to remember it. By the time Zachary grabs a pen and paper, each of them recollects a different number.}$$
$$	ext{All of the men agree that the first three numbers are 995.}$$
$$	ext{Three of them agree that the fourth number is 9.}$$
$$	ext{Three agree that the fifth number is 2.}$$
$$	ext{Three agree that the sixth number is 6; three others agree that the seventh number is also 6.}$$
$$	ext{Which of the numbers is most likely the telephone number of the pizzeria?}$$ A. $$	ext{995-9266}$$ B. $$	ext{995-9336}$$ C. $$995-9268$$ D. $$	ext{995-8266}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{All of the men agree that the first three numbers are 995. Three of them agree that the fourth number is 9. Three agree that the fifth number is 2. Three agree that the sixth number is 6; three others agree that the seventh number is also 6. "Option A" is the best choice because it is made up of the numbers that most of the men agree they saw.}$$

question: $$	ext{6. Mark is working with a realtor to find a location for the toy store he plans to open in his town. He is looking for a place that is either in, or not too far from, the center of town and one that would attract the right kind of foot traffic. Which of the following locations should Mark's realtor call to his attention?}$$ A. $$	ext{a storefront in a new high-rise building near the train station in the center of town whose occupants are mainly young, childless professionals who use the train to commute to their offices each day.}$$ B. $$	ext{a little shop three blocks away from the town's main street, located across the street from an elementary school and next door to an ice cream store}$$ C. $$	ext{a stand-alone storefront on a quiet residential street ten blocks away from the town's center}$$ D. $$	ext{a storefront in a small strip mall located on the outskirts of town that is also occupied by a pharmacy and a dry cleaner}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{This option is both near the center of town and in a location (near a school and an ice cream store) where children and their parents are sure to be around. This is the only option that meets both of Mark's requirements.}$$

question:
$$	ext{7. The neighborhood block association has received many complaints about people knocking on doors and soliciting money for an unknown charity organization even though door-to-door solicitation is prohibited by local laws. Three residents have provided descriptions of individuals who have come to their door asking for money.}$$

$$	ext{Solicitor \#1 is a white male, 20-25 years old, 5'9", 145 pounds, with very short brown hair. He was wearing a dark blue suit and carrying a brown leather briefcase.}$$

$$	ext{Solicitor \#2 is a white male, 25-30 years old, 6'2", 200 pounds, with a shaved-head. He was wearing a red T-shirt and jeans.}$$

$$	ext{Solicitor \#3 is a white male, approximately 23 years old, 5'10", slight build, with short brown hair. He was wearing a blue suit.}$$

$$	ext{Three days after the block association meeting, a resident noticed a man knocking on doors in the neighborhood and phoned the police to report the illegal activity. This solicitor was described as follows:}$$

$$	ext{Solicitor \#4 is a white male, 22 years old, 140 pounds, about 5'10", with short brown hair. He was carrying a briefcase and wearing a dark suit.}$$

$$	ext{Based on this description, which of the three solicitations was also likely carried out by Solicitor \#4?}$$ A. $$	ext{\#1, \#2, and \#3}$$ B. $$	ext{\#1, but not \#2 and \#3}$$ C. $$	ext{\#1 and \#3, but not \#2}$$ D. $$	ext{\#1 and \#2, but not \#3}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{The solicitor described as #2 has a shaved head and is much taller and heavier than the solicitors described as #1 and #3. Therefore, choices a and d, which include #2, can be ruled out. Solicitors #1, #3, and #4 have such similar descriptions that the correct answer is clearly choice c.}$$

question: $$	ext{8. Rita, an accomplished pastry chef who is well known for her artistic and exquisite wedding cakes, opened a bakery one year ago and is surprised that business has been so slow. A consultant she hired to conduct market research has reported that the local population doesn't think of her shop as one they would visit on a daily basis but rather a place they'd visit if they were celebrating a special occasion. Which of the following strategies should Rita employ to increase her daily business?}$$ A. $$	ext{making coupons available that entitle the coupon holder to receive a 25\% discount on wedding, anniversary, or birthday cakes}$$ B. $$	ext{exhibiting at the next Bridal Expo and having pieces of one of her wedding cakes available for tasting}$$ C. $$	ext{placing a series of ads in the local newspaper that advertise the wide array of breads}$$ D. $$	ext{moving the bakery to the other side of town}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{This is the only option that would encourage people to think of the bakery as a shop they would visit regularly and not just on special occasions.}$$

question: $$	ext{9. Dr. Miller has a busy pediatric dentistry practice and she needs a skilled, reliable hygienist to keep things running smoothly. The last two people she hired were recommended by top dentists in the area, but they each lasted less than one month. She is now in desperate need of a hygienist who can competently handle the specific challenges of her practice. Which one of the following candidates should Dr. Miller consider most seriously?}$$ A. $$	ext{Marilyn has been a hygienist for fifteen years, and her current employer, who is about to retire, says she is the best in the business. The clientele she has worked with consists of some of the wealthiest and most powerful citizens in the county.}$$ B. $$	ext{Lindy recently graduated at the top of her class from one of the best dental hygiene programs in the state. Prior to becoming a dental hygienist, Lindy spent two years working in a day care center.}$$ C. $$	ext{James has worked as a dental hygienist for three years in a public health clinic. He is very interested in securing a position in a private dental office.}$$ D. $$	ext{Kathy is an experienced and highly recommended dental hygienist who is also finishing up a degree in early childhood education, which she hopes will get her a job as a preschool teacher. She is eager to find a job in a pediatric practice, since she has always wanted to work with children.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The situation described indicates that Dr. Miller's practice presents some specific challenges, namely that it is a busy environment with a child clientele. There is also some indication that even highly recommended, experienced hygienists might not be cut out for Dr. Miller's office. There is nothing to suggest that Marilyn (choice a) or James (choice c) would be a good fit for Dr.Miller's practice.
