Kathy (choice d) has experience and she is also interested in working with children. However, the fact that she hopes to become a preschool teacher in the not-too-distant future indicates that she might not be the kind of committed, long-term employee that Dr. Miller needs. Lindy (choice b), with her hands-on experience working with children as well as a degree from a prestigious dental hygiene program, is the most attractive candidate for the position based on the situation described}$$

question: $$	ext{10. Mrs. Jansen recently moved to Arizona. She wants to fill her new backyard with flowering plants. Although she is an experienced gardener, she isn't very well-versed in what plants will do well in the Arizona climate. Also, there is a big tree in her backyard making for shady conditions and she isn't sure what plants will thrive without much direct sunlight. Her favorite gardening catalog offers several backyard seed packages. Which one should Mrs. Jansen choose?}$$ A. $$	ext{The Rainbow Collection is ideal for North-east gardens. It includes a variety of colorful perennials that thrive in cool, moist conditions.}$$ B. $$	ext{The Greenhouse Collection will blossom year after year if planted in brightly lit locations and watered regularly.}$$ C. $$	ext{The Treehouse Collection will provide lush green plants with delicate colorful flowers that thrive in shady and partially shady locations.}$$ D. $$	ext{The Oasis Collection includes a variety of perennials that thrive in dry climates and bright sunlight.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{The Treehouse Collection is the only package that can thrive in shady locations. Choice a requires a Northeastern climate. Choices b and d require bright sunlight.}$$

question: $$	ext{Vincent has a paper route. Each morning, he delivers 37 newspapers to customers in his neighborhood. It takes Vincent 50 minutes to deliver all the papers. If Vincent is sick or has other plans, his friend Thomas, who lives on the same street, will sometimes deliver the papers for him.}$$ A. $$	ext{Vincent and Thomas live in the same neighborhood.}$$ B. $$	ext{It takes Thomas more than 50 minutes to deliver the papers.}$$ C. $$	ext{It is dark outside when Vincent begins his deliveries.}$$ D. $$	ext{Thomas would like to have his own paper route.}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The fact that Vincent and Thomas live on the same street indicates that they live in the same neighborhood. There is no support for any of the other choices.}$$

question: $$	ext{The Pacific yew is an evergreen tree that grows in the Pacific Northwest. The Pacific yew has a fleshy, poisonous fruit. Recently, taxol, a substance found in the bark of the Pacific yew, was discovered to be a promising new anticancer drug.}$$ A. $$	ext{Taxol is poisonous when taken by healthy people.}$$ B. $$	ext{Taxol has cured people from various diseases.}$$ C. $$	ext{People should not eat the fruit of the Pacific yew.}$$ D. $$	ext{The Pacific yew was considered worthless until taxol was discovered.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Given the information presented, the only statement that could be considered true is that the fruit should not be eaten because it is poisonous. There is no support that taxol is poisonous or that taxol has cured anyone (choices a and b). There is no support for choice d.}$$

question: $$	ext{Erin is twelve years old. For three years, she has been asking her parents for a dog. Her parents have told her that they believe a dog would not be happy in an apartment, but they have given her permission to have a bird. Erin has not yet decided what kind of bird she would like to have.}$$ A. $$	ext{Erin's parents like birds better than they like dogs.}$$ B. $$	ext{Erin does not like birds.}$$ C. $$	ext{Erin and her parents live in an apartment.}$$ D. $$	ext{Erin and her parents would like to move.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Since Erin's parents think a dog would not be happy in an apartment, we can reasonably conclude that the family lives in an apartment. We do not know if Erin's parents dislike dogs (choice a) or if Erin dislikes birds (choice b). There is no support for choice d.}$$

question: $$	ext{Tim's commute never bothered him because there were always seats available on the train and he was able to spend his 40 minutes comfortably reading the newspaper or catching up on paperwork. Ever since the train schedule changed, the train has been extremely crowded, and by the time the doors open at his station, there isn't a seat to be found.}$$ A. $$	ext{Tim would be better off taking the bus to work.}$$ B. $$	ext{Tim's commute is less comfortable since the train schedule changed.}$$ C. $$	ext{Many commuters will complain about the new train schedule.}$$ D. $$	ext{Tim will likely look for a new job closer to home.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: The passage tells us that Tim's commute didn't bother him because he was always able to sit down and comfortably read or do paperwork. Therefore, it is reasonable to assume that Tim's commute has become less comfortable since the schedule change, because it is very crowded and he can no longer find a seat. There is no information given that supports choices a, c, and d.

question: $$	ext{5. When they heard news of the hurricane, Maya and Julian decided to change their vacation plans. Instead of traveling to the island beach resort, they booked a room at a fancy new spa in the mountains. Their plans were a bit more expensive, but they'd heard wonderful things about the spa and they were relieved to find availability on such short notice.}$$ A. $$	ext{Maya and Julian take beach vacations every year.}$$ B. $$	ext{The spa is overpriced.}$$ C. $$	ext{It is usually necessary to book at least six months in advance at the spa.}$$ D. $$	ext{Maya and Julian decided to change their vacation plans because of the hurricane.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{The first sentence makes this statement true. There is no support for choice a. The passage tells us that the spa vacation is more expensive than the island beach resort vacation, but that doesn't necessarily mean that the spa is overpriced; therefore, choice b cannot be supported. And even though the paragraph says that the couple was relieved to find a room on short notice, there is no information to support choice c, which says that it is usually necessary to book at the spa at least six months in advance.}$$

question: $$6. 	ext{Ten new television shows appeared during the month of September. Five of the shows were sitcoms, three were hour-long dramas, and two were news-magazine shows. By January, only seven of these new shows were still on the air. Five of the shows that remained were sitcoms.}$$ A. $$	ext{Only one of the news-magazine shows remained on the air.}$$ B. $$	ext{Only one of the hour-long dramas remained on the air.}$$ C. $$	ext{At least one of the shows that was cancelled was an hour-long drama.}$$ D. $$	ext{Television viewers prefer sitcoms over hour-long dramas.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{If there were seven shows left and five were sitcoms, this means that only two of the shows could possibly be dramas. Choices a and b may be true, but there is no evidence to indicate this as fact. The fact that all of the sitcoms remained does not necessarily mean that viewers prefer sitcoms (choice d).}$$

question: $$	ext{7. On weekends, Mr. Sanchez spends many hours working in his vegetable and flower gardens. Mrs. Sanchez spends her free time reading and listening to classical music. Both Mr. Sanchez and Mrs. Sanchez like to cook.}$$ A. $$	ext{Mr. Sanchez enjoys planting and growing vegetables.}$$ B. $$	ext{Mr. Sanchez does not like classical music.}$$ C. $$	ext{Mrs. Sanchez cooks the vegetables that Mr. Sanchez grows.}$$ D. $$	ext{Mrs. Sanchez enjoys reading nineteenth century novels.}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Because Mr. Sanchez spends many hours during the weekend working in his vegetable garden, it is reasonable to suggest that he enjoys this work. There is no information to suggest that he does not like classical music. Although Mrs. Sanchez likes to cook, there is nothing that indicates she cooks vegetables (choice c). Mrs. Sanchez likes to read, but there is no information regarding the types of books she reads (choice d).}$$

question: $$	ext{Georgia is older than her cousin Marsha. Marsha's brother Bart is older than Georgia. When Marsha and Bart are visiting with Georgia, all three like to play a game of Monopoly. Marsha wins more often than Georgia does.}$$ A. $$	ext{When he plays Monopoly with Marsha and Georgia, Bart often loses.}$$ B. $$	ext{Of the three, Georgia is the oldest.}$$ C. $$	ext{Georgia hates to lose at Monopoly.}$$ D. $$	ext{Of the three, Marsha is the youngest.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{If Georgia is older than Marsha and Bart is older than Georgia, then Marsha has to be the youngest of the three. Choice b is clearly wrong because Bart is the oldest. There is no information in the paragraph to support either choice a or choice c.}$$

question: $$	ext{Sara lives in a large city on the East Coast. Her younger cousin Marlee lives in the Mid-west in a small town with fewer than 1,000 residents. Marlee has visited Sara several times during the past five years. In the same period of time, Sara has visited Marlee only once.}$$ A. $$	ext{Marlee likes Sara better than Sara likes Marlee.}$$ B. $$	ext{Sara thinks small towns are boring.}$$ C. $$	ext{Sara is older than Marlee}.$$ D. $$	ext{Marlee wants to move to the East Coast.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Since the paragraph states that Marlee is the younger cousin, Sara must be older than Marlee. There is no information to support the other choices.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Tanya is older than Eric.}$$
$$	ext{Cliff is older than Tanya.}$$
$$	ext{Eric is older than Cliff.}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Because the first two statements are true, Eric is the youngest of the three, so the third statement must be false.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Blueberries cost more than strawberries.}$$
$$	ext{Blueberries cost less than raspberries.}$$
$$	ext{Raspberries cost more than strawberries and blueberries.}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Because the first two statements are true, raspberries are the most expensive of the three.}$$

question:
$$	ext{3. All the trees in the park are flowering trees.}\
	ext{Some of the trees in the park are dogwoods.}\
	ext{All dogwoods in the park are flowering trees.}\
	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{All of the trees in the park are flowering trees, So all dogwoods in the park are flowering trees.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Mara runs faster than Gail.}\
	ext{Lily runs faster than Mara.}\
	ext{Gail runs faster than Lily.}\
	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{We know from the first two statements that Lily runs fastest. Therefore, the third statement must be false.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Apartments in the Riverdale Manor cost less than apartments in The Gaslight Commons.}$$
$$	ext{Apartments in the Livingston Gate cost more than apartments in the The Gaslight Commons.}$$
$$	ext{Of the three apartment buildings, the Livingston Gate costs the most.}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Since the Gaslight Commons costs more than the Riverdale Manor and the Livingston Gate costs more than the Gaslight Commons, it is true that the Livingston Gate costs the most.}$$

question:
$$	ext{6. The Kingston Mall has more stores than the Galleria.}$$
$$	ext{The Four Corners Mall has fewer stores than the Galleria.}$$
$$	ext{The Kingston Mall has more stores than the Four Corners Mall.}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{From the first two statements, you know that the Kingston Mall has the most stores, so the Kingston Mall would have more stores than the Four Corners Mall.}$$

question:
$$	ext{7. All the tulips in Zoe's garden are white.}$$
$$	ext{All the pansies in Zoe's garden are yellow.}$$
$$	ext{All the flowers in Zoe's garden are either white or yellow}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{The first two statements give information about Zoe's tulips and pansies. Information about any other kinds of flowers cannot be determined.}$$

question:
$$	ext{8. During the past year, Josh saw more movies than Stephen.}$$
$$	ext{Stephen saw fewer movies than Darren.}$$
$$	ext{Darren saw more movies than Josh.}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{Because the first two sentences are true, both Josh and Darren saw more movies than Stephen. However, it is uncertain as to whether Darren saw more movies than Josh.}$$

question:
$$	ext{9. Rover weighs less than Fido.}\
	ext{Rover weighs more than Boomer.}\
	ext{Of the three dogs, Boomer weighs the least.}\
	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{According to the first two statements, Fido weighs the most and Boomer weighs the least.}$$

question:
$$	ext{10. All the offices on the 9th floor have wall-to-wall carpeting.}$$
$$	ext{No wall-to-wall carpeting is pink.}$$
$$	ext{None of the offices on the 9th floor has pink wall-to-wall carpeting.}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{If no wall-to-wall carpeting is pink and all the offices have wall-to-wall carpeting, none of the offices has pink wall-to-wall carpeting.}$$

question:
$$	ext{11.}$$
$$	ext{Class A has a higher enrollment than Class B.}$$
$$	ext{Class C has a lower enrollment than Class B.}$$
$$	ext{Class A has a lower enrollment than Class C.}$$
$$	ext{If the first two statements are true, the third statement is}$$ A. $$	ext{true}$$ B. $$	ext{false}$$ C. $$	ext{uncertain}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{From the first two statements, we know that of the three classes, Class A has the highest enrollment, so the third statement must be false.}$$

question: $$	ext{Senators from urban areas are very concerned about assuring that there will be funding for a new international airport. Senators from rural areas refuse to fund anything until money for agricultural subsidies is appropriated. If the legislature funds these two programs, on which of the following could they spend the rest of the money?}$$ A. $$	ext{the school music program and national radio}$$ B. $$	ext{hurricane preparedness}$$ C. $$	ext{harbor improvements and the school music program}$$ D. $$	ext{small business loan program}$$ E. $$	ext{national radio and senate office building remodeling}$$
answer:
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The total cost of the school music program and national radio is } \$1 	ext{ million, the amount left after the international airport and agricultural subsidies are funded.}$$
$$	ext{International airport + Agricultural subsidy program}$$
$$\$4 	ext{ million} + \$2 	ext{ million} = \$6 	ext{ million}$$
$$	ext{school music program and national radio is } \$1 	ext{ million.}$$
$$	ext{Hence, Total } \$7 	ext{ million.}$$

question: $$	ext{If the legislature decides to fund the agricultural subsidy program, national radio, and the small business loan program, what two other programs could they fund?}$$ A. $$	ext{harbor improvements and international airport}$$ B. $$	ext{harbor improvements and school music program}$$ C. $$	ext{hurricane preparedness and school music program}$$ D. $$	ext{hurricane preparedness and international airport}$$ E. $$	ext{harbor improvements and hurricane preparedness}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The only two programs that total 1.5 million dollars are the harbor improvements and school music program.}$$

question: $$	ext{If the legislature decides to fund the agricultural subsidy program, national radio, and the small business loan program, the only other single program that can be funded is}$$ A. $$	ext{hurricane preparedness.}$$ B. $$	ext{harbor improvements.}$$ C. $$	ext{school music program.}$$ D. $$	ext{senate office building remodeling.}$$ E. $$	ext{international airport.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{The total of the three programs (2 million + 0.5 million + 3 million) is 5.5 million. That leaves 1.5 million (7 million - 5.5 million), and the only single program needing that amount is the senate office building remodeling.}$$

question: $$	ext{One New York publisher has estimated that 50,000 to 60,000 people in the United States want an anthology that includes the complete works of William Shakespeare. And what accounts for this renewed interest in Shakespeare? As scholars point out, his psychological insights into both male and female characters are amazing even today.}$$ A. $$	ext{Shakespeare's characters are more interesting than fictional characters today.}$$ B. $$	ext{people even today are interested in Shakespeare's work because of the characters.}$$ C. $$	ext{academic scholars are putting together an anthology of Shakespeare's work.}$$ D. $$	ext{New Yorkers have a renewed interest in the work of Shakespeare.}$$ E. $$	ext{Shakespeare was a psychiatrist as well as a playwright.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The last sentence in the paragraph clearly gives support for the idea that the interest in Shakespeare is due to the development of his characters. Choice a is incorrect because the writer never makes this type of comparison. Choice c is wrong because even though scholars are mentioned in the paragraph, there is no indication that the scholars are compiling the anthology. Choice d is wrong because there is no support to show that most New Yorkers are interested in this work. There is no support for choice e either.}$$

question: $$	ext{One of the warmest winters on record has put consumers in the mood to spend money. Spending is likely to be the strongest in thirteen years. During the month of February, sales of existing single-family homes hit an annual record rate of 4.75 million.}$$ A. $$	ext{consumer spending will be higher thirteen years from now than it is today.}$$ B. $$	ext{more people buy houses in the month of February than in any other month.}$$ C. $$	ext{during the winter months, the prices of single-family homes are the lowest.}$$ D. $$	ext{there were about 4 million homes for sale during the month of February.}$$ E. $$	ext{warm winter weather is likely to affect the rate of home sales}.$$
answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{This is clearly the best answer because the paragraph directly states that warm weather affects consumers inclination to spend. It furthers states that the sales of single-family homes was at an all-time high. There is no support for choice a or c. Choice b is wrong because even though there were high sales for a particular February, this does not mean that sales are not higher in other months. Choice d presents a misleading figure of 4 million. The paragraph states that the record of 4.75 million was at an annual, not a monthly, rate.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Generation Xers are those people born roughly between 1965 and 1981. As employees, Generation Xers tend to be more challenged when they can carry out tasks independently. This makes Generation Xers the most entrepreneurial generation in history.}$$
$$	ext{This paragraph best supports the statement that Generation Xers}$$ A. $$	ext{work harder than people from other generations.}$$ B. $$	ext{have a tendency to be self-directed workers}$$ C. $$	ext{have an interest in making history}$$ D. $$	ext{tend to work in jobs that require risk-taking behavior.}$$ E. $$	ext{like to challenge their bosses work attitudes.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The support for choice b is given in the second sentence of the paragraph. Generation Xers like to work independently, which means they are self-directed. No support is given for either choice a or choice c. Choice d is not related to the paragraph. Although the paragraph mentions that Generation Xers like to be challenged, it does not say they like to challenge their bosses attitudes; therefore, choice e can be ruled out.}$$

question: $$	ext{This paragraph best supports the statement that}$$ A. $$	ext{fitness walking is a better form of exercise than weight lifting}.$$ B. $$	ext{a membership in a health club is a poor investment.}$$ C. $$	ext{walking outdoors provides a better workout than walking indoors.}$$ D. $$	ext{fitness walking is a convenient and valuable form of exercise.}$$ E. $$	ext{poorly designed athletic shoes can cause major foot injuries.}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{By stating that fitness walking does not require a commute to a health club, the author stresses the convenience of this form of exercise. The paragraph also states that fitness walking will result in a good workout. Choice a is incorrect because no comparison to weight lifting is made. Choice b may seem like a logical answer, but the paragraph only refers to people who are fitness walkers, so for others, a health club might be a good investment. Choice c is not in the passage. Although choice e seems logical, the paragraph does not indicate that the wrong shoes will produce major injuries.}$$

question: $$	ext{In the past, consumers would rarely walk into an ice cream store and order low-fat ice cream. But that isn't the case today. An increasing health consciousness combined with a much bigger selection of tasty low-fat foods in all categories has made low-fat ice cream a very profitable item for ice cream store owners.}$$ A. $$	ext{low-fat ice cream produces more revenue than other low-fat foods.}$$ B. $$	ext{ice cream store owners would be better off carrying only low-fat ice cream.}$$ C. $$	ext{ice cream store owners no longer think that low-fat ice cream is an unpopular item.}$$ D. $$	ext{low-fat ice cream is more popular than other kinds of ice cream.}$$ E. $$	ext{consumers are fickle and it is impossible to please them}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{This choice is supported as the best answer because the paragraph indicates that low-fat ice cream was once an unpopular item, but now, because consumers are more health conscious and because there is a wider array of tasty low-fat foods, low-fat ice cream is a profitable item for ice cream store owners. There is no indication that choices a, b, d, or e are true based on the information given.}$$

question: $$	ext{This paragraph best supports the statement that making school uniforms mandatory would increase a sense of community and harmony, instill discipline, and allow teachers and administrators to focus on more important issues rather than enforcing dress policies.}$$ A. $$	ext{inappropriate clothing leads to failing grades}.$$ B. $$	ext{students who wear school uniforms get into better colleges.}$$ C. $$	ext{teachers and administrators spend at least 25\% of their time enforcing the dress code}.$$ D. $$	ext{students are not interested in being part of a community}$$ E. $$	ext{school uniforms should be compulsory for high school students.}$$
answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The support for choice e is in the third sentence "we should make school uniforms mandatory". There is no evidence provided to support choices a, b, and d. And although we know that teachers and administrators are spending some of their time enforcing dress code, the paragraph does not quantify how much of their time is spent that way, so there is no support for choice c.}$$

question:
$$	ext{7. The criminal justice system needs to change. The system could be more just if it allowed victims the opportunity to confront the person who has harmed them. Also, mediation between victims and their offenders would give the offenders a chance to apologize for the harm they have done.}$$
$$	ext{This paragraph best supports the statement that victims of a crime should}$$ A. $$	ext{learn to forgive their offenders.}$$ B. $$	ext{have the right to confront their offenders.}$$ C. $$	ext{learn the art of mediation.}$$ D. $$	ext{insist that their offenders be punished.}$$ E. $$	ext{have the right to impose a sentence on their offenders.}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{This answer is clearly stated in the first sentence of the paragraph. There is no support in the passage for choices a, d, or e. As for choice c, although mediation is mentioned, the statement does not indicate that victims should be the mediators.}$$

question: $$	ext{A few states in this country are considering legislation that would prohibit schools from using calculators before the sixth grade. Other states take a different position. Some states are insisting on the purchase of graphing calculators for every student in middle school.}$$ A. $$	ext{there are at least two opinions about the use of calculators in schools.}$$ B. $$	ext{calculators are frequently a detriment to learning math.}$$ C. $$	ext{state legislators are more involved in education than ever before.}$$ D. $$	ext{the price of graphing calculators is less when schools buy in bulk.}$$ E. $$	ext{the argument against calculators in schools is unfounded.}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The paragraph clearly states that there are two differing opinions with regard to the use of calculators in the classroom. Although some people may believe that choice b is true, the paragraph does not indicate this. Choice c has no relation to the paragraph. Choice d makes logical sense, but the paragraph says nothing about cost. Choice e is an opinion that is not given in the paragraph.}$$

question:
$$	ext{9. Today's workforce has a new set of social values. Ten years ago, a manager who was offered a promotion in a distant city would not have questioned the move. Today, a manager in that same situation might choose family happiness instead of career advancement.}$$
$$	ext{This paragraph best supports the statement that}$$ A. $$	ext{most managers are not loyal to the corporations for which they work.}$$ B. $$	ext{businesses today do not understand their employees needs.}$$ C. $$	ext{employees social values have changed over the past ten years.}$$ D. $$	ext{career advancement is not important to today's business managers.}$$ E. $$	ext{companies should require their employees to accept promotions.}$$
answer: 答案是:C 解析如下: $$	ext{A change in employee social values over the past ten years is implied in the whole paragraph, but particularly in the first sentence. Choice a is incorrect because the loyalty of the managers to their corporations is never discussed. There is no support for choice b. In choice d, perhaps career advancement is less important than it once was, but the paragraph does not indicate that advancement is unimportant to managers. Choice e is an opinion that is not supported.}$$

question: $$	ext{This paragraph best supports the statement that}$$ A. $$	ext{the majority of the people in the world have never been seen by a doctor.}$$ B. $$	ext{food production in emerging nations has slowed during the past several years.}$$ C. $$	ext{most of the world's doctors are selfish about giving time and money to the poor.}$$ D. $$	ext{the medical-supply industry should step up production of its products.}$$ E. $$	ext{many people who live in emerging nations are not receiving proper medical care}$$
answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{This answer is implied by the statement that redistribution is needed so that people in emerging nations can have proper medical care. Choices a, b, and c are not mentioned in the passage. Choice d is also incorrect, the passage indicates that the distribution of medicine, not its production, is inadequate.}$$

答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The performance of the individual has to be tested over a span of time as the statement mentions. So, I is implicit. The statement mentions that the individual's worth shall be reviewed (during probation period) before confirmation. So, II is also implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: It is desirable to put the child in school at the age of 5 or so.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{flalign*}
&	ext{1. At that age the child reaches appropriate level of development and is ready to learn.}&\
&	ext{2. The schools do not admit children after six years of age.}&
\end{flalign*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Since the statement talks of putting the child in school at the age of 5, it means that the child is mentally prepared for the same at this age. So, I is implicit. But nothing about admission after 6 years of age is mentioned in the statement. So, II is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$3. 	ext{Statement: } 	ext{"In order to bring punctuality in our office, we must provide conveyance allowance to our employees." - In charge of a company tells Personnel Manager.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{flalign*}
&	ext{1. Conveyance allowance will not help in bringing punctuality.}&\
&	ext{2. Discipline and reward should always go hand in hand.}&
\end{flalign*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Assumption I goes against the statement. So, it is not implicit. The allowance will serve as a reward to the employees and shall provoke them to come on time. So, II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: Unemployment allowance should be given to all unemployed Indian youth above 18 years of age.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item There are unemployed youth in India who needs monetary support.
\item The government has sufficient funds to provide allowance to all unemployed youth.
\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{I directly follows from the statement and so is implicit. Also, the statement is a suggestion and does not tell about a government policy or its position of funds. So, II is not implicit.}$$

答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The mother warns her child with the expectation that he would stop troubling her. So, I is implicit. The general nature of children cannot be derived from the statement. So, II is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: The State government has decided to appoint four thousand primary school teachers during the next financial year.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item There are enough schools in the state to accommodate four thousand additional primary school teachers.
\item The eligible candidates may not be interested to apply as the government may not finally appoint such a large number of primary school teachers.
\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Such decisions as given in the statement are taken only after taking the existing vacancies into consideration. So, I implicit while II isn't.}$$

答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, the penalty is imposed to prevent people from misusing the alarm chain. This means that some people misuse it. So, I is implicit. The alarm chain is provided to stop the running train in times of urgency. So, II is also implicit.}$$

答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, nothing is mentioned about the professional nature of the job. So, I is not implicit. The statement hints that one rejects a thing that is easy to achieve. So, II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: The concession in rail fares for the journey to hill stations has been cancelled because it is not needed for people who can spend their holidays there.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{flalign*}
&	ext{1. Railways should give concession only to needy persons.}&\
&	ext{2. Railways should not encourage people to spend their holidays at hill stations.}&
\end{flalign*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement mentions that concessions should not be given to people who can afford to spend holidays in hill stations. This means they should be given only to needy persons. So, I is implicit. But, II does not follow from the statement and is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{10. Statement: "The bridge was built at the cost of Rs. 128 crores and even civil bus service is not utilizing it, what a pity to see it grossly underutilized." - A citizen's view on a new flyover linking east and west sides of a suburb. Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item The building of such bridges does not serve any public objective.
\item There has to be some accountability and utility of money spent on public projects.
\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, the statement expresses grave concern over a newly-built flyover not being utilized by public. This implies that such projects need to be taken up only after working out their utility and that the huge expenditure incurred on building such structures is worthwhile only if they prove useful for the public. Thus, only II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{11. Statement: The Government has decided to levy 2 percent on the tax amount payable for funding drought relief programmes.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{flalign*}
&	ext{1. The Government does not have sufficient money to fund drought relief programmes.}&\
&	ext{2. The amount collected by way of surcharge may be adequate to fund these drought relief programmes.}&
\end{flalign*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{Since a surcharge has been levied to fund drought relief programmes, it follows that the Government does not have sufficient money for the same. So, I is implicit. Besides, the percentage of surcharge must have been decided after studying the expected inflow in relation to amount of funds required. So, II is also implicit.}$$

question:
$$12. 	ext{Statement: Detergents should be used to clean clothes.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!\,$\item]
\item Detergents form more lather.
\item Detergents help to dislodge grease and dirt.
\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Nothing is mentioned about lather formation by the detergent. So, I is not implicit. Also, detergents should be used as they clean clothes better and more easily. So, II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{13. Statement: It will be a substantial achievement in the field of education if one provides one school for every village in our country and enforce attendance. Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item Children in villages do not attend school regularly.
\item Providing school to every village is desirable.
\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement lays stress on enforcing attendance. This implies that children in villages do not attend school regularly. So, I is implicit. Besides, the statement calls 'one school for every village' a 'substantial achievement'. So, II is also implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{14. Statement: The government has decided to disinvest large chunk of its equity in select public sector undertakings for a better fiscal management. Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate}
\item The amount generated out of the disinvestment process may reduce substantially the mounting fiscal deficits.
\item There will be enough demand in the market for the shares of these undertakings.
\end{enumerate}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The fact given in I directly follows from the phrase '..... for a better fiscal management' in the statement. So, I is implicit. However, the public response to the new policy cannot be ascertained. So, II is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{15. Statement: Never before such a lucid book was available on the topic.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{flalign*}
&	ext{1. Some other books were available on this topic.}&\
&	ext{2. You can write lucid books on very few topics.}&
\end{flalign*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{It follows from the statement that books on this topic were available before also but they were not 'lucid'. So, I is implicit. But a general comment as II cannot be made from the given statement. So, II is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{16. Statement: Please do not use lift while going down - an instruction on the top floor of a five-storey building. Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item While going down, the lift is unable to carry any load.
\item Provision of lift is a matter of facility and not of right.
\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement requests people not to use lift while moving down. This implies that the lift may be used to move up and the request has been made so that more people can use the lift for ascending which would otherwise cause more physical stress than going down the stairs. So, only II is implicit.}$$

答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The customer's eagerness to get the bills makes I implicit. Besides, the customer has written to the editor to bring the malfunctioning of the department to public notice. So, II is also implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{18. Statement: "This drink can be had either as it is, or after adding ice to it." - An advertisement. Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item People differ in their preferences.
\item Some people will get attracted to the drink as it can be had as it is.
\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The advertisement tells the different ways in which the drink can be had. This means that different people prefer to have it in a different way and that some people would prefer it only because it can be taken in a particular manner. So, both I and II are implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{19. Statement: Government has permitted unaided colleges to increase their fees. Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate}
\item Unaided colleges are in financial difficulties.
\item Aided colleges do not need to increase fees.
\end{enumerate}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$

question:

question:

question:
$$22. 	ext{Statement: Films have become indispensable for the entertainment of people.}~\
	ext{Assumptions:}\
 egin{enumerate}[label=oman*.]
\item 	ext{Films are the only media of entertainment.}~\
\item 	ext{People enjoy films.}~\
\end{enumerate}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Films are indispensable does not mean that they are the only means of entertainment. So, I is not implicit. Clearly, II follows from the statement. So, it is implicit.}$$

答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{Neither the volume of readership of the 'Times' in areas other than the Metropolis nor the reason for its huge acclamation is evident from the statement. So, neither I nor II is implicit.}$$

答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The performance of the individual has to be tested over a span of time as the statement mentions. So, I is implicit. The statement mentions that the individual's worth shall be reviewed (during probation period) before confirmation. So, II is also implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: It is desirable to put the child in school at the age of 5 or so.}$$

$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1.]
\item At that age the child reaches appropriate level of development and is ready to learn.

answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Since the statement talks of putting the child in school at the age of 5, it means that the child is mentally prepared for the same at this age. So, I is implicit. But nothing about admission after 6 years of age is mentioned in the statement. So, II is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: } 	ext{"In order to bring punctuality in our office, we must provide conveyance allowance to our employees." - In charge of a company tells Personnel Manager.}$$
$$	ext{Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item Conveyance allowance will not help in bringing punctuality.

answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Assumption I goes against the statement. So, it is not implicit. The allowance will serve as a reward to the employees and shall provoke them to come on time. So, II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: Unemployment allowance should be given to all unemployed Indian youth above 18 years of age.}$$

answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{I directly follows from the statement and so is implicit. Also, the statement is a suggestion and does not tell about a government policy or its position of funds. So, II is not implicit.}$$

question:

answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Such decisions as given in the statement are taken only after taking the existing vacancies into consideration. So, I implicit while II isn't.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: A warning in a train compartment - "To stop train, pull chain. Penalty for improper use Rs. 500."}$$

answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, the penalty is imposed to prevent people from misusing the alarm chain. This means that some people misuse it. So, I is implicit. The alarm chain is provided to stop the running train in times of urgency. So, II is also implicit.}$$

答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, nothing is mentioned about the professional nature of the job. So, I is not implicit. The statement hints that one rejects a thing that is easy to achieve. So, II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{10. Statement: "The bridge was built at the cost of Rs. 128 crores and even civil bus service is not utilizing it, what a pity to see it grossly underutilized." - A citizen's view on a new flyover linking east and west sides of a suburb. Assumptions:}$$

This implies that such projects need to be taken up only after working out their utility and that the huge expenditure incurred on building such structures is worthwhile only if they prove useful for the public. Thus, only II is implicit.}$$

Besides, the percentage of surcharge must have been decided after studying the expected inflow in relation to amount of funds required. So, II is also implicit.}$$

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:

answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, the statement encourages one to go to court to get his Provident Fund from his employer. This implies that the issue comes under the jurisdiction of courts and that it is the right of the employee to claim his Provident Fund. So, both I and II are implicit.}$$

question:

答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The use of the words 'impoverished community' in the statement makes I implicit while the phrase 'college education should be restricted to a brilliant few' makes II implicit.}$$

question:

question:

question:

question:

So, II is implicit. However, I cannot be assumed from the given statement and so it is not implicit.}$$

question:

question:

question:

Also, since intimacy and personal attention are required to treat addicts, it implies that addiction arises out of frustration due to strained relationships. So, II is also implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{41.}$$
$$	ext{Statement: Because of the large number of potholes in road X, reaching airport in time has become difficult.}$$

$$ egin{flalign*}
&	ext{1. Reaching airport in time may not be always necessary.}&\
&	ext{2. There is no other convenient road to the airport.}&
\end{flalign*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only assumption I is implicit}$$ B. $$	ext{Only assumption II is implicit}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II is implicit}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II is implicit}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II are implicit}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement presents the issue of 'not reaching airport in time' as a problem. This means that reaching airport in time is necessary. So, I is not implicit. Besides, it is mentioned that reaching airport in time has become difficult due to large number of potholes in road X. This implies that road X is the only possible way. So, II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{42. Statement: Safety and health practices in many Indian companies are well below the international standards. Assumptions:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate*}[!1]
\item International standards of health and safety are ideal and unrealistic.
\item Indian organizations do not consider safety and health management as their prime social responsibility.

answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement talks about the safety and health practices in Indian companies being far below international standards. It is clearly a criticism of Indian organizations not paying considerable attention to these aspects. So, II is implicit. The international standards demand perfection and are, in no way, non-achievable. So, I is not implicit.}$$

答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement stresses on the fact that though civic governance is the task of the municipal body, but all the tasks done come out to be more fruitful if the general public lends a helping hand in the same. So, only II is implicit.}$$

question:

answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, I mentions the aim for which the step talked about in the statement, has been undertaken while II mentions the essential requirement for it. So, both I and II are implicit.}$$

question:

answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement mentions that the company intends to provide accommodation only to outside candidates. This means that local candidates would have to arrange accommodation on their own and that the company may select local as well as outside candidates. Thus, only I is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{46. Statement: Cases of food poisoning due to consumption of liquor are increasing in rural areas. Assumptions:}$$

answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The statement talks of number of cases of food poisoning due to consumption of liquor and not of the number of cases consuming liquor. So, I is not implicit. Besides, the statement indicates that people in rural areas are getting spurious or low-grade liquor and no check is being kept on shops selling liquor there. So, II is implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{47. Statement: Lack of stimulation in the first four or five years of life can have adverse consequences. Assumptions:}$$

answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The lacking in first four or five years can be adverse because it is the main period of development. So, I is implicit. Since nothing is mentioned about the predictability of intelligence, II is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{48. Statement: The X-Airlines has decided to increase the passenger fare by 15 percent with immediate effect.}$$

&	ext{2. Other airline companies may also hike the passenger fares.}&

answer: 答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, such decisions are taken only after making sure that it will not affect the company's business adversely. So, I is implicit. However, the impact of this increase on other airlines cannot be ascertained. So, II is not implicit.}$$

question:
$$	ext{49. Statement: Shalini made an application to the bank for a loan of Rs. 1,80,000 by mortgaging her house to the bank and promised to repay it within five years. Assumptions:}$$

answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The fact that Shalini has applied for a loan of Rs. 1,80,000 implies that the bank can grant a loan above Rs. 1,00,000. So, I is implicit. II also follows directly from the statement and so is implicit.}$$

question:

answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, prevention from mosquitoes and elimination of mosquitoes are two ways to prevent malaria. So, both the courses follow.}$$

question:
$$	ext{Statement: Severe drought is reported to have set in several parts of the country.}$$

answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{In the break-out of a natural calamity, the basic duty of the government becomes to provide the basic amenities essential to save the lives of people and cattle. Providing financial assistance to all would put undue burden on the country's resources. So, only II follows.}$$

question:

question:

question:
$$	ext{Statement: Most of those who study in premier engineering colleges in India migrate to developed nations for better prospects in their professional pursuits.}$$
$$	ext{Courses of Action:}$$
$$ egin{enumerate}
\item All the students joining these colleges should be asked to sign a bond at the time of admission to the effect that they will remain in India at least for ten years after they complete education.
\item All those students who desire to settle in the developed nations should be asked to pay entire cost of their education which the government subsidises.
\end{enumerate}$$ A. $$	ext{Only I follows}$$ B. $$	ext{Only II follows}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II follows}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II follows}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II follow}$$
answer: 答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, no student can be bound to live and work in the country against his wish. So, I does not follow. However, it is quite right to recover the extra benefits awarded to students if they do not serve their own country. So, II follows.}$$

答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{Clearly, increased migration would add to the burden on city's infrastructure. So, attempts should be made to make the villagers feel comfortable in the villages itself. So, only course I follows.}$$

答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{The exodus can be stopped by providing the people conditions conducive to living. So, both the courses follow.}$$

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:

question:
$$	ext{Statement: The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased.}$$

$$ egin{flalign*}
&	ext{1. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose high import duty on these fruits, even if these are not of good quality.}&\
&	ext{2. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits would be increased.}&\
\end{flalign*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only I follows}$$ B. $$	ext{Only II follows}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II follows}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II follows}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II follow}$$
answer: 答案是:D 解析如下: $$	ext{The ideas suggested in both I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.}$$
答案是:A 解析如下: $$	ext{The increase may not be a permanent one. So, it's better not to open new colleges but increase seats in the existing colleges. So, only I follow.}$$
答案是:B 解析如下: $$	ext{The accidents can clearly be prevented by barring people from crossing the tracks when the gates are closed, So, only II follows.}$$

question:

question:

question:

answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: $$	ext{To ensure that debts taken are repaid promptly, the customer's requirements and future prospects ought to be studied and their work constantly checked. Thus, both the courses follow.}$$

question:
$$	ext{30. Statement: A very large number of students have failed in the final high school examination due to faulty questions in one of the subjects.}$$
$$	ext{Courses of Action:}$$

\end{enumerate*}$$ A. $$	ext{Only I follows}$$ B. $$	ext{Only II follows}$$ C. $$	ext{Either I or II follows}$$ D. $$	ext{Neither I nor II follows}$$ E. $$	ext{Both I and II follow}$$

question:

question:

question:

question:
